findings.title: 14.780 candidate not reproducible from committed source: load-bearing model edits are uncommitted
findings.intended: The estimated candidate should be regenerable from a version-controlled state of the evaluation code (income process, bequest normalization, new moment, extended polish API).
findings.implemented: All combined-spec machinery exists only as working-tree diffs to five tracked files (local_panel.py, solver.py, calibration.py, parameters.py, cli.py). At HEAD, run_local_polish has no additional_search_bounds/additional_targets/additional_weights/fixed_model_overrides/normalize_bequest_utility kwargs, so the drivers raise TypeError.
findings.economic_consequence: No version-controlled provenance for the headline calibration; any accidental checkout, stash, or the nightly backup committing a different state silently orphans the result.
findings.decisive_test: git stash; run tmp/overnight_combined_20260710/run_overnight_combined.py --max-evals 1 → TypeError on run_local_polish kwargs; git stash pop.
findings.evidence: Dirty local_panel.py:529-533 adds 'normalize_bequest_utility: bool = False, additional_search_bounds: ... additional_targets: ... additional_weights: ... fixed_model_overrides:' to run_local_polish; git diff shows these lines as '+' only. Driver run_overnight_combined.py:155-158 passes 'normalize_bequest_utility=True, additional_search_bounds=[("H0", 1.0, 10.0)], additional_targets={...}'.

findings.title: Override merge-order flip vs HEAD: income now wins over profile; HEAD would compute a different economy
findings.intended: The Rouwenhorst income objects (z_grid, z_weights, Pi_z, persistence) must govern the solve.
findings.implemented: Dirty tree: '**base_overrides(...), **(extra_overrides or {}), **income, **theta' (lines 849-852). HEAD: '**income, **(extra_overrides or {})' (795-797), so the production profile's tracked stale 5-point grid (production_profile.py:136-141 'z_grid: PRODUCTION_Z_GRID.copy() ... Pi_z: make_persistent_transition_matrix(') would override Rouwenhorst.
findings.economic_consequence: Reproduction attempts from HEAD (or any tree without this one-line flip) run a different income process and produce a different loss surface while appearing to use the same spec; this is a silent divergence, not a crash.
findings.decisive_test: Solve one tiny case (Nb<=40) with the two merge orders and compare income_process_fingerprint and the loss; lead already verified the dirty-tree order reproduces 14.780020699972585 exactly.
findings.evidence: grep dirty local_panel.py:849-852: '**base_overrides(J=J, Nb=Nb, n_house=n_house, max_iter_eq=max_iter_eq), / **(extra_overrides or {}), / **income, / **theta,'. git show HEAD:...local_panel.py lines 795-798: '**base_overrides(...), / **income, / **(extra_overrides or {}), / **theta,'.

findings.title: All overnight drivers and result artifacts are outside version control
findings.intended: Search drivers, launch scripts, and best-candidate artifacts for a headline calibration should be committed or archived under version control.
findings.implemented: git status shows '?? tmp/' (whole directory), '?? code/model/tools/run_intergen_combined_recalibration.py', '?? code/cluster/submit_intergen_combined_recalibration.sh', '?? code/cluster/submit_intergen_combined_spec_test.sh'; output/ is ignored via .gitignore ('output/'), so output/model/combined_recalibration/overnight_20260710_report/* (current_bound_best.json etc.) is unversioned.
findings.economic_consequence: A single 'rm -rf tmp' or clean checkout destroys the only local copy of the programs that produced the result; the torch snapshot copy is currently unreachable (no SSH).
findings.decisive_test: git ls-files tmp/ code/model/tools/run_intergen_combined_recalibration.py returns empty; SHA256 pins recorded in hashes.json.
findings.evidence: git status --porcelain: '?? code/model/tools/run_intergen_combined_recalibration.py', '?? tmp/' ; .gitignore: 'output/'. Hashes: run_overnight_combined.py sha256 aa15e99026a6cdc1..., current_bound_best.json 77651d72e41344ac... (full values in hashes.json).

findings.title: Repo driver and tmp overnight drivers are materially different programs; relaxed-bounds arms lose their diagnostic_only marker in wave 2
findings.intended: One canonical driver per spec; diagnostic (relaxed-bounds) arms clearly separated from calibrated arms.
findings.implemented: Repo driver caps 'max_evals=min(args.max_evals, 160)' and 'minutes=min(args.minutes, 60.0)' (run_intergen_combined_recalibration.py:54,60); wave 1 ran 850 evals/330 min with jitter ladder (0..0.15) and per-arm relaxed bounds installed by mutating the module global 'lp.GLOBAL_DE_BOUNDS = list(bounds)' (run_overnight_combined.py:116); wave-1 metadata sets 'diagnostic_only: args.arm in {preference_relaxed, housing_relaxed}' (line 123) but run_wave2_lateral.py maps global_preference/global_housing onto the same relaxed bounds (lines 45-49) and writes no diagnostic_only flag; housing_relaxed_best is reported alongside current_bound_best in the report artifacts.
findings.economic_consequence: housing_relaxed_best could be read as a calibrated candidate although it was searched under out-of-spec bounds; and rerunning the repo driver cannot replicate the overnight search intensity.
findings.decisive_test: Read overnight_20260710_report/summary.json and housing_relaxed_best.json theta against PRODUCTION_SEARCH_BOUNDS: any parameter outside production bounds proves the relaxed-bounds provenance (search-design specialist scope).
findings.evidence: run_overnight_combined.py:71-77: 'elif arm == "housing_relaxed": replacements = {"h_bar_0": (0.25, 6.00), "h_bar_jump": (0.05, 3.50), "h_bar_n": (0.02, 3.00), "chi": (0.40, 1.60)}'; line 116 'lp.GLOBAL_DE_BOUNDS = list(bounds)'; run_wave2_lateral.py:45-49 arm map; repo driver lines 54,60 'min(args.max_evals, 160)' / 'min(args.minutes, 60.0)'.

findings.title: write_best_case_diagnostics solves without profile and fixed-spec overrides (panel path only)
findings.intended: Best-case diagnostic re-solves should use the identical override stack as the ranked record.
findings.implemented: Diagnostic re-solve merges only '**base_overrides(...), **income, **theta' — no extra_overrides (production profile) and no fixed_model_overrides (q, delta, eta_supply, normalize_bequest_utility). Only run_local_panel calls it (line 264); run_local_polish, the overnight path, does not, so the 14.780 loss is unaffected.
findings.economic_consequence: Any diagnostics packet generated through run_local_panel for a combined-spec candidate would silently be solved under the legacy finance/supply/bequest spec, misleading visual inspection.
findings.decisive_test: Run run_local_panel with the combined overrides on a tiny grid and compare the diagnostics-path solve's P.q, P.delta, P.normalize_bequest_utility against the ranked record's.
findings.evidence: local_panel.py:1303-1307: 'overrides = { **base_overrides(J=J, Nb=Nb, n_house=n_house, max_iter_eq=max_iter_eq), **income, **theta, }' inside write_best_case_diagnostics.

findings.title: Torch snapshot byte-identity unverified (SSH blocked)

findings.title: Entire quantitative section of the paper is the stale 16.13 interim calibration, superseded by 14.78 on the identical objective
findings.intended: Paper reports the best available estimate under the combined corrected spec (15 targets, candidate_replacement_post_audit_v1 weights).
findings.implemented: Every number matches output/model/intergen_circulation_candidate_20260711 (loss 16.1319) digit-for-digit; the 14.78 current_bound_best (same targets, identical weights verified element-by-element) is not reflected anywhere.
findings.economic_consequence: All parameter estimates, moments, mechanism magnitudes, and policy effects are 1.35 loss units off the frontier. Fit narratives flip sign at 14.78 (own_rate 0.509 under-target becomes 0.653 over-target; childlessness 0.220 over becomes 0.171 under; old_age_own_rate worsens 0.889 to 0.948), and the at-bound parameter pattern changes (psi_child, kappa_fert become interior; h_bar_0 and effectively H0 at 9.9997/10 hit bounds), so identification commentary is also stale.
findings.decisive_test: diff evidence/free_parameters.csv and evidence/target_fit.csv in intergen_circulation_candidate_20260711 against the paper tables (exact match), then against overnight_20260710_report/current_bound_best_*.csv (systematic mismatch).
findings.evidence: Paper line 994 '\beta_{\mathrm{annual}} ... 0.949917' and line 999 '\bar h_{\mathrm{jump}} ... 1.776823' == free_parameters.csv 'beta_annual,...,0.9499167945' and 'h_bar_jump,...,1.7768232150'; paper line 1071 '2.419 & 1.854' == target_fit.csv 'prime30_55_childless_owner_minus_renter_mean_rooms,2.41876173,1.8544488717232248'; 14.78 report has 1.9477049699027535 for the same moment.

findings.title: Headline policy magnitudes come from a battery whose own summary records Baseline validation: FAIL and labels results fixed-theta diagnostics; paper reports one closure column without disclosing the others
findings.intended: Policy effects (+3.4%, +4.9% births; +0.66%, -18.80% price) presented as the model's population-adjusted counterfactuals.
findings.implemented: Numbers are the 'dp_pct fixed' and fixed-scale births-index columns of output/model/corrected_policy_battery_loss16/from_loss16_2267_tight (baseline loss 16.131919, same 16.13 theta). Source README line 8: 'Baseline validation: `FAIL`'; README: 'Losses are fixed-theta diagnostics, not recalibrated SMM losses' and QBAR_STAR/aggregation are 'D8 decision inputs'. Same cases table carries 'dp_pct scaled' +1.5616/-17.7987 and 'published dp_pct' +1.3366/-11.1621, unmentioned in the paper.
findings.economic_consequence: The grant's price effect varies by factor ~2.4 (+0.66 vs +1.56) and the package's by ~1.7 (-18.80 vs -11.16) across closure conventions in the very same output table; the paper's specific magnitudes are convention-dependent and stale-theta. All four policy numbers must be withheld pending re-run at 14.78 with the closure choice stated. (Validation FAIL is against very tight tolerances: price rel gap 9.36e-5 vs tol 1e-6; scale gap 4.2e-4 vs 1e-8.)
findings.decisive_test: Read from_loss16_2267_tight/README.md cases table and summary.json baseline_validation_checks; match paper +0.66/-18.80 to the 'dp_pct fixed' column and note the adjacent scaled/published columns.
findings.evidence: README.md:8 '- Baseline validation: `FAIL`.'; cases table row cf_grant_A0p4_Hge6: 'dp_pct fixed +0.6629 ... published dp_pct +1.3366'; row cf_package_tax2_grant: '-18.8026 ... -11.1621'; summary.json 'baseline_price_relative_gap: 9.356762064236554e-05, baseline_price_tolerance: 1e-06'.

findings.title: build_intergen_mechanics_packet.py applies the combined fixed spec only via an opt-in CLI flag and ignores the combined_corrected_spec flag stored in the candidate record
findings.intended: Re-solving a combined-spec candidate record reproduces its spec: Rouwenhorst income, q=(1.02)^4-1, delta4, eta_supply=1.75, normalize_bequest_utility=True, patched aggregate-rooms target.
findings.implemented: The spec block is gated on args only: resolve_grid line 384 sets '"combined_corrected_spec": bool(args.combined_corrected_spec)' and never reads source_grid['combined_corrected_spec'] even though candidate JSONs carry it (circulation candidate grid has combined_corrected_spec: true). Without the flag the else-branch uses income_process_overrides(states) whose default process is "current" (local_panel.py:1033), stale q/delta/eta, unnormalized bequests, and drops the rooms-target patch (lines 80-82). Also hR_max falls back to 8.0 (line 372) vs production 6.
findings.economic_consequence: One omitted flag silently rebuilds every paper packet/figure/mechanism statistic under the wrong model (wrong income process, wrong user cost, wrong supply elasticity, wrong bequest utility, effectively 14-moment loss) with no error — the exact N_HOUSE=6 landmine class. Any future 14.78 paper refresh run without --combined-corrected-spec would be invisibly wrong.
findings.decisive_test: Run the packet builder on circulation_candidate_best.json with and without --combined-corrected-spec at tiny Nb and compare recovered loss/price to the record (with-flag reproduces 16.1319/0.781613; without-flag does not).
findings.evidence: resolve_grid: '"combined_corrected_spec": bool(args.combined_corrected_spec),' (line 384); solve_candidate: 'if bool(grid.get("combined_corrected_spec", False)) else income_process_overrides(int(grid["income_states"]))' (lines 426-427); 'hR_max": float(args.hR_max if args.hR_max is not None else source_grid.get("hR_max", 8.0))' (line 372); local_panel.py:1033 'process: str = "current"'.

findings.title: regen_lifecycle_compare_notitle.py and run_policy_counterfactuals_from_record.py re-solve through the dt_cp two-location strand and cannot apply any element of the combined intergen spec
findings.intended: Regenerating a lifecycle figure or policy counterfactual from a saved candidate reproduces the candidate's model specification.
findings.implemented: Both scripts route through build_direct_calibration_setup('benchmark', ...) from the dt_cp direct-calibration strand (make_full_dispersion_diagnostic_note.build_setup lines 233-255: geo_weight, population_closure, E_C, r_bar_C two-location objects). Neither passes q, delta, eta_supply, normalize_bequest_utility, Rouwenhorst parameters, or H0-in-theta; regen defaults --hR-max to 8.0 (line 20); run_policy keeps only hR_max/alpha_cons/owner_*/tenure_choice_kappa/H_own from the record.
findings.economic_consequence: If either is pointed at an intergen combined-spec record to refresh the paper's lifecycle figure or policy table, it silently solves a different model (different strand, different income process, different user cost, cap 8 instead of 6). They did NOT produce the current paper artifacts (frozen R/python scripts in intergen_circulation_candidate_20260711 and the tmp phase9b driver did), but they are the discoverable in-repo tools with paper-figure-shaped names.
findings.decisive_test: grep both scripts for rouwenhorst/eta_supply/normalize_bequest (zero hits) and confirm build_setup calls build_direct_calibration_setup with two-location E_C/r_bar_C.
findings.evidence: regen_lifecycle_compare_notitle.py:20 'parser.add_argument("--hR-max", type=float, default=8.0)'; make_full_dispersion_diagnostic_note.py:237 'return build_direct_calibration_setup("benchmark", geo_weight=250.0, population_closure="outside_option_benchmark_normalized", ...)'; solve_record lines 268-269 'P.E_loc = np.array([float(P.E_loc[0]), theta_dict["E_C"]])'.

findings.title: Cache-based plotters default to a pre-combined-spec June-27 cache or a nonexistent path and perform no spec validation
findings.intended: Policy-slice/overlay figures plot the active calibrated solution.
findings.implemented: plot_intergen_policy_overlay.py DEFAULT_CACHE points at output/model/intergen_postaudit_sample3h_review_20260627/best_globalde_task1_packet/solution_cache.pkl (June 27, pre-combined-spec); plot_intergen_policy_slice.py DEFAULT_CACHE is output/model/intergen_model_run_current/solution_cache.pkl, which does not exist on disk. Neither checks the cache's spec/vintage against anything.
findings.economic_consequence: A no-argument overlay run silently plots a stale-objective solution as if current; the slice tool's default crashes. Vintage confusion risk when refreshing supplemental figures, not a correctness bug in existing paper artifacts.
findings.decisive_test: ls output/model/intergen_model_run_current (No such file or directory); run overlay with no args and inspect payload provenance.
findings.evidence: overlay lines 30-31: 'DEFAULT_PACKET = ROOT / "output/model/intergen_postaudit_sample3h_review_20260627/best_globalde_task1_packet"; DEFAULT_CACHE = DEFAULT_PACKET / "solution_cache.pkl"'; slice line 21-22: 'DEFAULT_RUN_DIR = ROOT / "output" / "model" / "intergen_model_run_current"'; shell: 'ls: output/model/intergen_model_run_current: No such file or directory'.

findings.title: Untracked paper-issues ledger is stale on BQ-2 and HF-4 relative to the audited combined-spec object
findings.intended: Ledger reflects the current paper/code state so open issues are triaged correctly.
findings.implemented: BQ-2 states the zero-estate bequest recentering 'was not included in the subsequent code repairs' and 'the paper was changed to describe the code'; but the paper now displays the recentered form (paper line 508 '(\theta_1+\max\{W^B,0\})^{1-\sigma}-\theta_1^{1-\sigma}') and both the 16.13 packet and the 14.78 overnight spec set normalize_bequest_utility=True. HF-4 states 'the active code fixes ... \bar H=4 and the elasticity at \eta=1', but the combined spec fixes eta=1.75 (Saiz) and estimates H0 (7.51 at 16.13; 9.9997 near bound 10 at 14.78).
findings.economic_consequence: A reader of the ledger would wrongly treat the bequest normalization as an outstanding pre-estimation blocker and the supply block as uncalibrated; conversely the ledger misses the genuinely new issue that H0 is pinned at its upper bound in the 14.78 candidate, and misses the policy-battery validation FAIL.
findings.decisive_test: Compare ledger BQ-2/HF-4 text against paper line 508, the packet command '--combined-corrected-spec' in intergen_circulation_candidate_20260711/README.md, and normalize_bequest_utility=True in build_intergen_mechanics_packet.py line 424.
findings.evidence: Ledger line 34-35: 'This omission was identified in the July 9 audit, but it was not included in the subsequent code repairs.'; ledger line 16: 'The active code fixes the supply level at \(\bar H=4\) and the elasticity at \(\eta=1\).'; packet builder line 424: '"normalize_bequest_utility": True,'; paper line 961-962: 'Housing-supply elasticity $\eta$ & 1.75'.

findings.title: normalize_bequest_utility defaults False; 8 intergen re-solve tools and most CLI subcommands silently revert combined-spec records to the old unnormalized bequest
findings.lines: 66; solver.py:5611; cli.py:158,266; tools listed in evidence
findings.intended: Any re-solve of a combined-spec record (diagnostics, Jacobians, policy counterfactuals, solver-accuracy checks) reproduces the estimated model, i.e. the normalized bequest B(0,n)=0 plus the combined fixed spec (q, delta4, eta_supply, H0, Rouwenhorst).
findings.implemented: P.normalize_bequest_utility = False by default and solver reads getattr(P, "normalize_bequest_utility", False). Tools audit_intergen_sensitivity_jacobian.py:316-320, audit_intergen_final_best_pathologies.py:146-148, audit_intergen_solver_accuracy.py:206-208, audit_intergen_parent_credit_margin.py, audit_intergen_mechanism_grid.py, compare_intergen_income_processes.py:203-208, run_intergen_code_repair_comparison.py, run_intergen_policy_poc.py:193-203 all build overrides = {**base_overrides, **income_process_overrides, **theta} with no flag and none of the combined fixed-spec overrides. CLI subcommands smoke/solve/diagnostics/local-panel/global-de-panel cannot set the flag at all; only local-polish has --normalize-bequest-utility (store_true, default False).
findings.economic_consequence: Replaying the overnight best through any of these entry points drops terminal utility by theta0*(1+theta_n*n)/theta1 = 13.18 (n=0), 23.30 (n=1), 33.43 (n=2) utils at the live candidate theta — a family-size-dependent level shift that changes fertility and saving policies, so replayed moments, Jacobians, and policy counterfactuals will not match the estimated model. Any post-estimation diagnostic or policy number produced by these tools for the combined spec is contaminated until they are patched or fed explicit overrides.
findings.decisive_test: Solve the combined-spec theta at Nb<=40 once via audit_intergen_sensitivity_jacobian.solve_theta and once via the overnight override dict; assert getattr(P, "normalize_bequest_utility") differs (False vs True) and moments diverge.
findings.evidence: parameters.py:66 "P.normalize_bequest_utility = False"; solver.py:5611 "normalize = bool(getattr(P, \"normalize_bequest_utility\", False))"; audit_intergen_sensitivity_jacobian.py:316-320 "overrides = { **base_overrides(J=int(args.J), Nb=int(args.Nb), n_house=int(args.n_house), max_iter_eq=int(args.max_iter_eq)), **income_process_overrides(int(args.income_states)), **theta, }"; cli.py:158 "polish.add_argument(\"--normalize-bequest-utility\", action=\"store_true\")"; rg count of "normalize_bequest_utility" is 0 in all 8 tools.

findings.title: Per-evaluation record (best.json) does not store the bequest flag or resolved overrides; only run-level meta does
findings.intended: A saved best record is self-describing enough to replay the exact estimated specification.
findings.implemented: run_local_panel_case returns theta/moments/losses/p_eq/timings but no overrides and no normalize_bequest_utility; the flag and fixed_model_overrides live only in the run-level meta (local_panel.py:621 '"normalize_bequest_utility": bool(normalize_bequest_utility)', 624 '"fixed_model_overrides": jsonable(fixed_model_overrides or {})').
findings.economic_consequence: Tools that consume a record alone (exactly the BQ-1 tools) cannot recover the spec from the record; combined with the False default this operationalizes the BQ-1 trap and undermines reproducibility from the record artifact.
findings.decisive_test: json.load best.json from the overnight outdir and assert 'normalize_bequest_utility' not in record while it is in the sibling summary/meta.
findings.evidence: local_panel.py:879-894 return dict keys: case, label, status, rank_loss, full_old_nonlocation_loss, theta, moments, p_eq, market_residual, strict_converged, tol_eq, elapsed_sec, timings, income_process_fingerprint — no overrides/flag.

findings.implemented: Lines 5067-5076 now contain owner-rooms moment accounting ('owner_mass_3055_childless += mo' etc.); bequest_utility_vec is at 5604-5620.

findings.evidence: solver.py:5067 "owner_mass_3055_childless += mo"; solver.py:5604 "def bequest_utility_vec(b, nk, P):".

findings.title: With theta1=0.01 fixed, the normalized bequest motive saturates at tiny estates, weakening marginal identification of theta0/theta_n
findings.intended: theta_n captures how completed family size scales the marginal value of the estate, identified by differential parent saving/housing at death.
findings.implemented: At sigma=2, B is bounded above by theta0*(1+theta_n*n)/theta1 and equals 90% of that bound at W=0.09, 98% at W=0.5 (model wealth is order 1). Marginal utility falls from 10^4*scale at W=0 to ~scale at W=1. At the live candidate (theta0=0.1318, theta_n=0.7679) the bound is 13.2-33.4 utils.
findings.economic_consequence: The bequest term operates mostly as an extensive-margin level jump in parents' terminal value rather than a saving-slope shifter, making (theta0, theta_n) near-substitutable with any other parenthood terminal-value shifter; theta_n's estimate may be pinned by moments unrelated to bequest behavior. Interpretation caveat, not a code bug; also note theta_n multiplies the encoded index n (per-child effects are half the reported theta_n).
findings.decisive_test: Small-grid Jacobian of the 15 target moments w.r.t. theta1 in {0.01, 0.1, 0.5} holding theta0/theta1 and theta0*theta_n/theta1 fixed; if moments barely move, the curvature is unidentified and theta_n is a level parameter.
findings.evidence: solver.py:5617-5619 "utility = (P.theta1 + b) ** (1 - P.sigma) / (1 - P.sigma)" ... "utility = utility - P.theta1 ** (1 - P.sigma) / (1 - P.sigma)"; parameters.py:65 "P.theta1 = 0.01"; production_profile.py:51-52 searches only theta0 [0,2] and theta_n [0,1.5].

findings.title: Both reported candidates descend from a single wave-1 seed; the wave-2 'global' LHS stage never escaped that basin
findings.intended: A global-basin exploration: 60 Latin-hypercube draws over each full arm cube, with the wave-1 seed injected as draw 0, so the reported optima are supported as global over the stated boxes.
findings.implemented: units[0] = wave-1 seed ('units[0] = source_unit', line 83); polish seeds from the global-stage best. In BOTH winning arms the global best was units[0]: the two final candidates share beta=0.8411423842686492, c_bar_n=0.4559787014242588, theta0=0.1318301350511569 to 16 digits across independent task seeds (impossible for independent LHS draws), and all differing coordinates decompose exactly into pattern steps (H0 diff 1.44 = 2x0.08x9; alpha_cons 0.044 = 1x0.08x0.55; kappa_fert 0.114048 = 1x(0.08x0.6^4)x11). Dispersed points are ~4% of ~39k evals (21 wave-1 jittered starts + <=1,416 LHS draws in 14 dimensions).
findings.economic_consequence: The reported parameter estimates are local optima along one descent lineage, not evidence about the global loss surface over the stated bounds; multi-modality (e.g. a different beta/theta0 basin) is untested. Any text calling these 'global' or treating the 13.90-vs-14.78 arm comparison as a bounds-relaxation experiment with independent optima overstates what the search established.
findings.decisive_test: On scratch: grep the two winning tasks' global/cases.jsonl for the minimum strict rank_loss among lhs_001..lhs_059 vs lhs_000; confirm lhs_000 (seed) is the global-stage best in global_current/task_1 and global_housing/task_17.
findings.evidence: run_wave2_lateral.py:83 'units[0] = source_unit'; report summary.json: current_bound_best theta beta=0.8411423842686492, c_bar_n=0.4559787014242588, theta0=0.1318301350511569 == housing_relaxed_best same three values; H0 9.999672059746388 vs 8.559672059746388 (diff exactly 1.44); check_search_integrity_output.json step_decomposition {H0: '2 x step(0.08)', kappa_fert: '1 x step(0.010368)', alpha_cons: '1 x step(0.08)'}

findings.title: current_bound_best is a silently box-clipped projection of a diagnostic-only relaxed-arm wave-1 seed; its three at-bound parameters are clipping artifacts
findings.intended: A seed theta outside the arm's search box should be rejected or flagged before seeding a production-bounds search.
findings.implemented: global_unit_from_theta ends 'return np.clip(unit, 0.0, 1.0)' (line 966) — out-of-box thetas are silently projected onto the box (verified: out_of_box_seed_silently_clipped=true; None only for missing keys). select_seed (run_overnight_combined.py:45-58) takes the strict min across ALL wave-1 arms including the two arms wave-1 launch metadata marks diagnostic_only=True (line 123). current_bound_best sits exactly at c_bar_0=1.28 (upper), chi=1.15 (upper), h_bar_0=1.0 (lower) while housing_relaxed_best has chi=1.2006>1.15 and h_bar_0=0.921<1.0 — h_bar_0<1 is representable only in the housing_relaxed box, so the shared seed almost surely came from the wave-1 housing_relaxed (diagnostic-only) arm.
findings.economic_consequence: The 'current-bounds' candidate's boundary parameters reflect projection of a relaxed-box optimum, not that the production-box search independently drove them to the bounds; the diagnostic-only label on the relaxed arms leaked into the production-bounds candidate's lineage. The point itself is a valid strict optimum within production bounds, but interpreting its at-bound c_bar_0/chi/h_bar_0 as calibration verdicts (rather than evidence that the production box binds) is wrong.
findings.decisive_test: On scratch: read global_current/task_1/lateral_summary.json source_seed_path and the seed record's theta; confirm it lies in the wave-1 housing_relaxed arm with h_bar_0<1 and/or chi>1.15.
findings.evidence: local_panel.py:966 'return np.clip(unit, 0.0, 1.0)'; run_overnight_combined.py:47 glob '**/task_*/best.json' (all arms), :123 '"diagnostic_only": args.arm in {"preference_relaxed", "housing_relaxed"}'; report summary.json current_bound_best at_bound=true for c_bar_0 (1.28), chi (1.15), h_bar_0 (1.0) vs housing_relaxed_best chi=1.2006166273226997, h_bar_0=0.9210468183699152

findings.title: meta['source_controlled_bounds'] is mislabeled under runtime bounds patching and contradicts production_profile_spec.bounds in relaxed-arm metadata
findings.intended: Metadata should record the actual search box and, separately, the source-controlled production box, each correctly labeled.
findings.implemented: '"source_controlled_bounds": [... for name, lo, hi in GLOBAL_DE_BOUNDS]' reads the module global AFTER the drivers patch it (run_overnight_combined.py:116, run_wave2_lateral.py:52 'lp.GLOBAL_DE_BOUNDS = list(bounds)'), so for relaxed arms this field shows the RELAXED box labeled as source-controlled, while meta['production_profile_spec']['bounds'] (line 634 -> production_profile.py:116-119) shows the unpatched PRODUCTION_SEARCH_BOUNDS. meta['bounds'] (641-644) correctly records the actual search box.
findings.economic_consequence: Relaxed-arm metadata.json on scratch contains two contradictory boxes plus a false label; a reader reconstructing the run from 'source_controlled_bounds' would infer the relaxed box is in source control. Reproducibility itself is unharmed because meta['bounds'] is accurate, and the report CSVs list the correct per-arm bounds (housing_relaxed_best_parameters.csv shows chi<=1.6, h_bar_0>=0.25, h_bar_jump<=3.5, h_bar_n<=3.0).
findings.decisive_test: Read any wave-1 preference_relaxed or housing_relaxed task metadata.json on scratch and compare its source_controlled_bounds entries against PRODUCTION_SEARCH_BOUNDS in production_profile.py:39-53.
findings.evidence: local_panel.py:645-648 '"source_controlled_bounds": [{"name": name, ...} for name, lo, hi in GLOBAL_DE_BOUNDS]'; run_overnight_combined.py:116 'lp.GLOBAL_DE_BOUNDS = list(bounds)'; production_profile.py:38 comment 'These are the source-controlled bounds that generated the reported July runs.'

findings.title: Wave-2 crash path when polish yields no strict record raises AttributeError instead of the intended RuntimeError
findings.intended: If the polish stage produces no strict-converged best, fall back to the global-stage best or fail with the explicit RuntimeError 'wave-2 task ended without a strict best'.
findings.implemented: 'final_best = polish_best if lp.is_better_record(polish_best, best) else best' — polish.get('best') is None when run_local_polish finds zero strict records (local_panel.py:811), and record_selection_loss(None) executes None.get('strict_converged') (local_panel.py:1152 via 1142) raising AttributeError before line 156's guard.
findings.economic_consequence: Fail direction is still a crash (no result contamination, no silent masking) but the task dies with a confusing traceback and discards its valid strict global-stage best; it can only trigger if the polish seed — already strict at the global stage — fails to re-converge, which is unlikely for a deterministic re-evaluation.
findings.decisive_test: python -c: import run_wave2_lateral's lp; call lp.is_better_record(None, {'strict_converged': True, 'rank_loss': 1.0}) and observe AttributeError.
findings.evidence: run_wave2_lateral.py:154 'final_best = polish_best if lp.is_better_record(polish_best, best) else best'; local_panel.py:1133 'candidate_loss = record_selection_loss(record)', :1142 'return bool(record.get("strict_converged", False))'

findings.title: Wave-2 global-stage truncation cannot be excluded from local evidence (and would further shrink the already-thin LHS coverage)
findings.intended: All 60 LHS draws per task evaluated, giving the stated (already sparse) cube coverage.
findings.implemented: 'global_deadline = start + min(100.0, args.minutes * 0.28) * 60.0' with minutes=330 gives 92.4 min; 'if idx > 0 and time.perf_counter() >= global_deadline: break'. At 37 s/solve 60 draws need ~37 min and even at 74 s/solve ~74 min, so truncation requires average case time >~92 s — plausible for non-convergent random LHS points burning max_iter_eq=10 plus scalar refinement. The per-task 'global_completed' counter is written only to scratch lateral_summary.json (lines 158-173), unreachable (SSH blocked).
findings.economic_consequence: If truncated, the effective global coverage per task falls below 60 draws in 14 dimensions, further weakening any global claim (S1); no effect on the validity of the reported optima themselves.
findings.decisive_test: On scratch: 'jq .global_completed */task_*/lateral_summary.json' under overnight_20260710_wave2_lateral and check for values < 60.
findings.evidence: run_wave2_lateral.py:86 'global_deadline = start + min(100.0, args.minutes * 0.28) * 60.0'; :89 'if idx > 0 and time.perf_counter() >= global_deadline: break'; run_wave2.sh:29-30 '--global-draws 60 --local-evals 720 --minutes 330'

findings.title: Smoke-run execution for the combined wave is unverifiable locally; wave-1 seed root ($OVERNIGHT_SEED_ROOT) is not recorded in any local file
findings.intended: Per Long-Run Search Safety, the exact loop structure is smoke-tested before launch, and the search design (including what seeded wave 1) is recoverable.
findings.implemented: Correctly-shaped smoke paths exist: run_array.sh:21-25 OVERNIGHT_SMOKE=1 branch (4 arms, MAX_EVALS=2, MINUTES=12, JITTER=0) and run_wave2_smoke.sh (3 arms, --global-draws 2 --local-evals 2 --minutes 12, seeds 2026071190+ID). But execution evidence (slurm logs, smoke outdirs) is cluster-side only, and OVERNIGHT_SEED_ROOT is a submit-time env var (run_array.sh:13 'SEED_ROOT="${OVERNIGHT_SEED_ROOT:?}"') whose value appears nowhere locally; transcripts show a prior strict tree stage3_onehour_from_17_5912 (~17.14 best) and an in-flight incumbent 16.100969 as circumstantial seed-provenance evidence.
findings.economic_consequence: The full provenance chain seed_root -> wave-1 seed -> both reported candidates cannot be certified from the local tree alone; the smoke also would not have exercised the 92.4-min deadline logic (S5) even if it ran.
findings.decisive_test: On scratch: read any wave-1 task overnight_launch.json (records source_seed_path and source_seed_loss) and the slurm submit lines / smoke logs for jobs around 13313018 and 13314033.
findings.evidence: run_array.sh:13 'SEED_ROOT="${OVERNIGHT_SEED_ROOT:?}"'; run_overnight_combined.py:124-125 '"source_seed_path": source_path, "source_seed_loss": source_record["rank_loss"]' (written only under the task outdir on scratch); run_wave2_smoke.sh:24 '--global-draws 2 --local-evals 2 --minutes 12'

findings.title: Hard-targeted median moment is grid-discrete (uninterpolated b-grid value)
findings.intended: Weighted median of old nonhousing wealth / annual income, ages 65-75, as a smooth SMM target (target 2.23046078, weight 0.8).
findings.implemented: weighted_quantile returns q[idx] = values[pos] with no interpolation ('pos = np.searchsorted(cw, prob)... q[idx] = values[pos]'), and the underlying ratio values are b-grid nodes divided by a constant annual pension (all 66-74 households are retired with identical flat pension, solver.py:4894-4896), so the moment takes values only on a ~120-point discrete grid and is piecewise-constant in parameters.
findings.economic_consequence: The objective has plateaus and jumps in the directions that move old wealth (beta, theta0, theta_n), degrading pattern/Nelder-Mead search and making the reported miss (model 1.03 vs 0.38 across the two candidates against target 2.23; 8-20% of loss) partly an artifact of step response. Violates the project rule 'Never hard-target median moments (grid-discrete)' and the June 22 surrogate finding that discrete median moments break smooth search.
findings.decisive_test: At the current_bound_best theta, perturb beta_annual by +/-0.5% on Nb<=40 and plot old_nonhousing_wealth_to_income_median_6575: it will be a step function while the mean counterpart moves smoothly.
findings.evidence: utils.py:228-231: 'pos = np.searchsorted(cw, prob, side="left") ... q[idx] = values[pos]'; solver.py:4965-4967: 'stats.old_nonhousing_wealth_to_income_median_6575 = weighted_median_from_cells(old_nonhousing_ratio_vals, old_nonhousing_ratio_wts)'; solver.py:4896: 'yj = annual_gross_income_at_state(P, i, jj, 1.0)' (constant across the all-retired 66-74 window).

findings.title: Newly searched supply parameter H0 sits at its upper bound while its identifying moment undershoots
findings.intended: H0 added as a 14th free parameter in [1,10], identified by the new aggregate_mean_occupied_rooms_18_85 target (5.7800, weight 6).
findings.implemented: current_bound_best has 'H0,9.999672059746388,...,near_bound_2pct,True' while the rooms moment is 5.5511 vs target 5.7800 (gap -0.229); the optimum is bound-constrained, not interior. Combined with c_bar_0 at 1.28 (upper), h_bar_0 at 1.0 (lower), chi at 1.15 (upper) and tenure_choice_kappa at 0, four to five of the 14 parameters are at bounds.
findings.economic_consequence: The nominal 15-moment/14-parameter just-identification count is misleading: the effective interior problem is ~9-10 parameters, and the supply scale the spec was built to discipline is not actually pinned inside the allowed region — the model wants more housing supply than the bound permits, so the aggregate-rooms target is unreached for a constraint reason, not an economic one. Any comparative-static or policy use of H0=10 inherits the bound, not the data.
findings.decisive_test: Re-run the polish with H0 bound extended to [1,20] for a few dozen evals: if H0 moves materially past 10 and the rooms gap closes, the estimate is bound-constrained (per CLAUDE.md, at-bound parameters must be reported as such).
findings.evidence: current_bound_best_parameters.csv: 'H0,9.999672059746388,9.999672059746388,1.0,10.0,True,False'; run_overnight_combined.py:156-158: 'additional_search_bounds=[("H0", 1.0, 10.0)], additional_targets={"aggregate_mean_occupied_rooms_18_85": ROOMS_TARGET}, additional_weights={...: 6.0}'; fit CSV: 'aggregate_mean_occupied_rooms_18_85,5.779970481941968,5.551107552668675,-0.22886...'

findings.title: theta_n still identified by a single ~2%-of-loss moment; D11 weak identification not resolved
findings.intended: Bequest tilt theta_n internally identified by the old parent-childless nonhousing wealth gap (target 1.00744952, weight 2.0), with theta0 now interior (0.1318) supposedly resolving D11.
findings.implemented: The gap moment contributes 0.81-0.83% of loss in both candidates and is the only moment that separates theta_n from theta0/psi_child/kappa_fert; the two near-equal-loss candidates (14.78 vs 13.90) carry theta_n = 0.768 vs 1.109 (45% apart) while the gap moment sits at nearly the same value (1.251 vs 1.247). June 18 Jacobian audit (predates this spec) found cos(psi_child,theta_n)=0.975, cos(kappa_fert,theta_n)=-0.946 and tiny theta0/theta_n column norms.
findings.economic_consequence: Intergenerational/bequest counterfactuals that depend on theta_n are not disciplined: materially different bequest-tilt economies fit the 15-moment system equally well. theta0 being interior removes the theta0~0 pathology but does not pin the parent-childless tilt.
findings.decisive_test: Fix theta_n at 0.77 and at 1.11 with all else re-polished briefly (tiny grid): if losses stay within ~1 of each other, theta_n is set-identified at best under this target system.
findings.evidence: calibration.py:96: '"old_parent_childless_nonhousing_wealth_to_income_gap_6575": 1.00744952'; :420: weight '2.0'; recomputation output: contribution 0.119/14.78 = 0.81% and 0.115/13.90 = 0.83%; parameters CSVs: theta_n 0.7679202774006243 vs 1.109009045400624 with theta0 identical 0.1318301350511569 in both.

findings.title: Systematic age-window drift: all labeled data windows resolve to older model windows
findings.intended: Moments conditioned on data age windows 25-34, 25-35, 30-55, 65-75, 18-85.
findings.implemented: age_to_index = clip(round((age-18)/4), 0, J-1) on the 18+4j grid maps 25-34 and 25-35 to indices 2-4 (period-start ages 26,30,34; coverage 26-38), 30-55 to indices 3-9 (30-54; coverage 30-58), 65-75 to indices 12-14 (66-74; coverage 66-78), and the 18_85 aggregate covers 18-86. No .5 rounding tie is hit, so this is grid coarseness, not an off-by-one bug; index 12 (age 66) is exactly J_R so the old window is 100% retirees.
findings.economic_consequence: Every windowed target is matched against a model population 1-3 years older than its data label; own_rate_2534 in particular contains no age-25 mass, which mechanically raises the model moment relative to a true 25-34 sample. Consistent across moments so comparisons are internally coherent, but level targets embed the drift.
findings.decisive_test: Recompute own_rate over indices 1-4 (ages 22-34) vs 2-4 on a tiny solve and compare to the 0.3412 target to bound the drift's contribution to the -9.1pp miss at current_bound_best.
findings.evidence: solver.py:4615: 'idx = int(round((float(age) - float(P.age_start)) / max(float(P.da), 1e-12)))'; :4832-4834: 'early_mass = float(np.sum(g[:, :, :, a25s : a34e + 1, :, :])) ... stats.own_rate_2534 = early_owner / max(early_mass, 1e-12)' with a25s=2, a34e=4.

findings.title: Two inconsistent 'childless' definitions across targeted moments; young-wealth cell includes empty-nest parents
findings.intended: PSID 'young childless renters 25-35' (plausibly never-parents) for M10; ACS childless-in-household for room moments; PSID ever-parent vs childless for the old wealth gap.
findings.implemented: current_child_bin_dt returns bin 2 ('childless') when 'cs == 0 or cs > dep_last', so room and young-wealth moments count stochastic empty-nest parents (child-maturity hazard 1/4.5 per period from age 18-22 births) as childless; the old-age wealth gap instead uses nn>0 vs (nn==0 and cs==0), a never-parent definition.
findings.economic_consequence: M10's model cell includes 26-34 year-old parents whose model child already matured — households with different saving histories than the PSID never-parent sample — biasing the model young wealth ratio and its sensitivity to fertility parameters; small mass but nonzero, and the definitional split across moments complicates interpreting the parent-childless contrasts jointly.
findings.decisive_test: On a tiny solve, recompute M10 restricting to nn==0 & cs==0 and compare to the bin-2 version; the difference bounds the contamination.
findings.evidence: solver.py:5740-5744: 'if cs == 0 or cs > dep_last: return 2 ... if current_n <= 0: return 2'; :4432: 'if childless_only and current_child_bin_dt(nn, cs, dep_last) != 2: continue'; :4929: 'elif cs == 0:' (old-age childless branch under nn==0).

findings.title: TARGET_MOMENT_OBJECTS ledger is stale on the old-wealth denominators (code already divides by annual income)
findings.intended: The measurement ledger should describe the implemented statistic so calibration reports and target revisions are trustworthy.
findings.implemented: Ledger says old_parent_childless_..._gap_6575 is 'ratio-of-sums: nonhousing wealth divided by model period income' and old_nonhousing_wealth_to_income_median_6575 'should divide by annual income' (status needs-fix), but solver.py:4896 uses annual_gross_income_at_state (annual pension) and :4938-4961 computes weighted means of per-state ratios, not ratio-of-sums. The fix is in HEAD; the ledger was not updated.
findings.economic_consequence: No effect on the estimated loss; risk is future misreporting — an agent trusting the ledger would 'fix' an already-fixed denominator or misdescribe the moment in the paper.
findings.decisive_test: Read solver.py:4894-4967 against calibration.py:267-278 (done); a one-line ledger update after the audit closes it.
findings.evidence: calibration.py:270-271: '"model": "parent minus childless ratio-of-sums: nonhousing wealth divided by model period income, ages 65-75.", ... "status": "needs-fix"' vs solver.py:4896: 'yj = annual_gross_income_at_state(P, i, jj, 1.0)' and :4945: 'return numerator / max(denominator, 1e-12)' (weighted mean of ratios).

findings.title: Weight vector is scale-ad-hoc and old_age_own_rate (w=160) decides the estimate
findings.intended: An SMM weighting reflecting the informativeness/precision of each moment across mixed units (rates, rooms, wealth ratios).
findings.implemented: Hand-set constants inherited across target-set versions (e.g. old_age_own_rate 160 from CANDIDATE_REPLACEMENT_OLD_RETENTION_V1_WEIGHTS, calibration.py:459; the 3plus rooms moment inherits housing_increment_1to2's weight 8 by construction, :500-502) with no variance or sensitivity justification anywhere in the file. Recomputed shares: old_age_own_rate is 36.5% (current_bound) and 45.5% (housing_relaxed) of the loss; the bottom five moments jointly are under 3%.
findings.economic_consequence: The reported 'best' theta is largely the theta that minimizes the old-age ownership overshoot; moments with small weights (old wealth median 0.8, gap 2.0) are effectively free to miss by factors of 2-6, so fit claims and cross-candidate rankings (14.78 vs 13.90) are weight-vector artifacts as much as economics. Not recommending dropping targets; recommending the weights be documented or diagonal-GMM-justified before the calibration is called final.
findings.decisive_test: Recompute both candidates' losses under equal weights or inverse-target-squared weights (script trivially extends recompute_loss.py): if the ranking of the two candidates flips, the selection between them is weight-driven.
findings.evidence: calibration.py:489: '"old_age_own_rate": 160.0'; :494-503 (post-audit weights built by dict surgery from the 14-moment set); recompute output: 'old_age_own_rate 160.0 0.183565 5.391359 36.48%' and '160.0 0.198796 6.323147 45.48%'.

findings.title: Paper reports the frozen circulation candidate (loss 16.132), not the overnight strict best (loss 14.780)

findings.implemented: Exact transcription of output/model/intergen_circulation_candidate_20260711 (pattern_i003_d08_m, case 96 of source/task13_cases.jsonl, rank_loss 16.13191908615311, residual 2.3904e-05, Nb=120); current_bound_best (14.780020699972585, strict, reproduced by lead) has H0=9.9997 at the upper bound 10 vs paper H-bar=7.51, beta_ann 0.9577 vs 0.949917, psi_child 0.269 vs 0.35, theta0 0.1318 vs 0.2215, and a different fit (old-age own 0.948 vs 0.889, childless 0.171 vs 0.220, mean rooms 5.551 vs 5.188)

findings.title: Fit table omits moment weights (0.8 to 160) that drive the objective
findings.intended: Full target-fit table with weights per the project reporting standard
findings.implemented: Target and model columns only; weights range from 0.8 (old wealth median) to 160 (old-age ownership), so the reported 'misses' have very unequal objective weight
findings.economic_consequence: Readers cannot see that old-age ownership dominates the objective (2.48 of 16.13 loss) while the largest raw miss (old wealth, -1.53) is nearly unweighted
findings.decisive_test: Compare paper table to packet/target_fit.csv weight column (done)

findings.title: July 2 age-window finding still present: own_rate_2534 and old_age_own_rate_6575 windows shifted vs data windows (weights 80 and 160)
findings.intended: Model ownership moments measured over the data windows ages 25-34 (ACS) and 65-75.
findings.implemented: age_to_index rounds (age-18)/4: a25s=2, a34e=4, a65s=12, a75e=14, so own_rate_2534 integrates model ages {26,30,34} and old_age_own_rate_6575 integrates {66,70,74}. Under the 4-year-bin reading ([18+4j,18+4j+4)) the old window drops age 65 (bin j=11) and includes 75-77 (bin j=14), and the young window covers 26-38; under the point-age reading the chosen indices are the correct nearest points. Code unchanged since the July 2 finding.
findings.economic_consequence: Level bias in two of the three highest-weighted ownership moments (80 and 160) and in the derived old_minus_young_owner_rate_6575_2534, if the data moments are bin-aligned; theta compensates, distorting the lifecycle ownership fit interpretation. Likely order 1-3pp on each moment.
findings.decisive_test: Adjudicate the data-side age convention for the ACS/HRS windows; if bin-aligned, recompute both moments with j in [11,14] (old) and [1,4] (young) at current_bound_best theta and compare loss contributions. T6 of output/model/full_audit_20260711/scripts/timing_identity_test.py prints the exact windows used.

Test output: 'own_rate_2534 uses j in [2,4] -> age bins [26,30) [30,34) [34,38)'; 'old_age_own_rate_6575 uses j in [12,14] -> age bins [66,70) [70,74) [74,78)'.

findings.title: fert_by_age and mean_age_first_birth computed on POST-fertility-split childless mass (biased birth-flow weights); diagnostic-only in the active target set
findings.intended: Birth flow at age j = (pre-choice childless mass at j) x P(birth at j); mean age at first birth weighted by that flow.
findings.implemented: The KFE mutates g at fertile ages in place before stats ('g[:, :, :, j, zz, 0, 0] = 0.0'), so the (0,0) cell at age j holds only households who CHOSE no birth. compute_statistics then applies the same-age birth probabilities again: weight = (pre-split mass) x fp0 x (1-fp0) instead of (pre-split mass) x (1-fp0).
findings.economic_consequence: mean_age_first_birth and fert_by_age are tilted toward ages where fp0(1-fp0) is large. Not in the active 15-moment set (mean_age_first_birth dropped from all replacement/no_timing sets), but reported in extract_moments (calibration.py:1133) and enters the reported diagnostic full_old_nonlocation_loss (local_panel.py:858, weight 12). Pre-dates the July 9 repair; identical in both timing arms.
findings.decisive_test: Place unit childless mass at one fertile age in a tiny model and compare stats.fert_by_age[j] against E[n' choice] computed from the pre-split mass with fert_probs[...,j]; they differ by the factor fp0.
findings.evidence: solver.py:3776-3777 'g[:, :, :, j, zz, 0, 0] = 0.0' / 'g[:, :, :, j, zz, :, :] += gpf'; solver.py:4801-4806 'gs = g[:, ten, i, j, 0, 0] ... pb = 1 - fp[nz, ten, i, j, 0]; wb = float(np.sum(gs[nz] * pb)); tba += ra * wb'.

findings.title: housing_increment_0to1_eventstudy_t3 has two different definitions across the two KFE implementations (active markov path = DiD vs control; non-markov path = post minus pre, no control cohort)
findings.intended: One moment definition: birth cohort housing minus a no-birth counterfactual at the same horizon (the calibration target maps to this name, calibration.py:1134).
findings.implemented: Markov KFE (active production path): '(birth_es3_post_sum - birth_es3_control_post_sum) / birth_es3_mass' with an explicitly propagated childless control cohort. Non-markov KFE: 'birth_es3_post_sum / birth_es3_mass - birth_es3_pre_sum / birth_es3_mass' - no control-cohort accumulator exists in forward_distribution at all. The docstring comment 'at horizon 0 the control is the pre-birth state' is inexact under repaired timing (the control gets current-period realized choices; the pre-birth mean uses inherited holdings).
findings.economic_consequence: Any tool or comparison run through the non-markov/income-type path reports a different statistical object under the same moment name, contaminating cross-specification comparisons of the housing-response moment. The July 10 overnight used the markov path, so the calibrated results are unaffected.
findings.decisive_test: Solve the same tiny model through both paths (use_income_types markov vs single-type) and print housing_increment_0to1_eventstudy_t3; values differ whenever childless controls buy/upsize in the birth period.
findings.evidence: solver.py:3659-3661 'stats.housing_increment_0to1_eventstudy_t3 = ( birth_es3_post_sum / birth_es3_mass - birth_es3_pre_sum / birth_es3_mass ...)'; solver.py:4070-4072 'stats.housing_increment_0to1_eventstudy_t3 = ( (birth_es3_post_sum - birth_es3_control_post_sum) / birth_es3_mass ...)'; rg shows 'birth_es3_control_post_sum' only at 3730 (init), 3857, 4071 - all in/for the markov function.

findings.title: Replaying stored pre-repair records replays legacy timing through their saved switches (reproduction semantics, bookkeeping hazard)
findings.intended: All headline/policy numbers computed under repaired timing.
findings.implemented: The legacy path is fully retained behind the flag (solver.py:4015-4029) and FROZEN_SOURCE_REPRO_SWITCHES pins use_postdecision_current_distribution=False / propagate_birth_entry_grant=False for the comparison arm; tools that replay a record's stored overrides verbatim (e.g. tools/run_policy_counterfactuals_from_record.py, the compare_* tools) will faithfully rerun old timing for pre-July-9 records.
findings.economic_consequence: Risk of mixing old-timing and new-timing numbers in packets/paper if a pre-repair record is replayed without noticing; no effect on the July 10 overnight (verified to run with the repaired switches via production_profile_overrides merge, local_panel.py:565-579 and 848-853).
findings.decisive_test: grep any record JSON feeding a headline table for 'use_postdecision_current_distribution': false.
findings.evidence: production_profile.py:76-79 '"use_postdecision_current_distribution": False, ... "propagate_birth_entry_grant": False'; parameters.py:100-101 defaults 'P.propagate_birth_entry_grant = True' / 'P.use_postdecision_current_distribution = True' (so tools omitting the switch get repaired timing - the task-brief hypothesis of a False default is wrong on this tree).

findings.title: Owner housing surplus: code chi*(H-hbar) vs paper chi*H-hbar
findings.intended: Paper: Stone-Geary utility over (h - hbar(n,s)) with owner services h = chi_O*H_k (full_draft.tex:509 '(\mathbf h-\bar h(n,s))^{1-\alpha}', :521 '\chi_O H_k, & d=O_k'), composing to surplus chi*H - hbar.
findings.implemented: Code multiplies chi into the NET surplus: kernels.py:915 'ht_c = hsv - owner_h_bar_scale * hbc' then :918 'ht_c = owner_service_premium * ht_c', i.e. chi*(H - hbar). No code comment claims the paper's form; only the paper claims it.
findings.economic_consequence: At the candidate (chi=1.15, hbar=3.46 for a one-child family) rung H=4 gives paper surplus 1.14 vs code 0.62 (~2x); code also makes rungs with H<hbar infeasible even when chi*H>hbar. Changes the level and family-size gradient of the ownership premium and the interpretation of chi; the ESTIMATED model is the code object, so this is a paper-documentation error unless the paper form was intended, in which case the estimates are of the wrong model.
findings.decisive_test: Textual: the two quoted lines are unambiguous. If the paper form is intended, a one-line kernel change (apply chi to hsv before subtracting hbar) and recalibration; compare owner-rate-by-family-size moments.
findings.evidence: kernels.py:915-918: 'ht_c = hsv - owner_h_bar_scale * hbc ... ht_c = owner_service_premium * ht_c'; solver.py:2012: 'owner_service_premium = max(float(getattr(P, "chi", 1.0)), 1e-8)'; full_draft.tex:521: '\chi_O H_k, & d=O_k,' and :509: '(\mathbf h-\bar h(n,s))^{1-\alpha}'

findings.title: H0 pinned at its upper search bound 10; rooms target unreachable within [1,10]
findings.intended: H0 searched in [1,10] so the new aggregate_mean_occupied_rooms_18_85 target 5.779970 identifies the supply scale as an interior SMM parameter.
findings.implemented: current_bound_best has H0=9.999672059746388 (at bound); p_eq=0.68976 gives user_cost=0.11430 and supply factor (uc/r_bar)^1.75=0.555, so max attainable rooms=5.55 vs target 5.78; H0 needed at that price is 10.41. Rooms model value 5.5511, gap -0.2289, loss contribution 6*gap^2=0.314 of total 14.780. housing candidate interior at H0=8.56 but also under target (-0.195).
findings.economic_consequence: The calibration is a bound-constrained optimum, not an interior SMM point: the rooms moment cannot discipline its designated parameter, effective parameter/moment count each drop by ~1, and the residual rooms misfit spills into the other 14 moments. Root cause is the supply pivot r_bar=0.16 inherited from the old finance regime (old p_init=r_bar/uc=0.556 vs new 0.966): under the new user-cost rate the same demand clears at uc*p far below r_bar, deflating supply for any H0.
findings.decisive_test: Re-polish the candidate with H0 bound widened (e.g. [1,14]) or with r_bar re-pivoted to the new user_cost_rate; if H0 moves above 10 and the rooms gap closes with lower loss, the bound was binding and the reported candidate is not a local optimum of the unconstrained problem. Cluster-side; not run (SSH unavailable).
findings.evidence: run_overnight_combined.py:156: 'additional_search_bounds=[("H0", 1.0, 10.0)]'; repro/current_repro_exact.json: theta.H0=9.999672059746388, p_eq=0.6897624769313723, rooms=5.551107552668675; check output: supply_predicted_from_formula=5.550946 (matches rooms to 2.9e-5=residual), H0_needed_for_target_at_this_price=10.412; solver.py:5484: 'supply = P.H0 * (user_cost / P.r_bar) ** P.xi_supply'

findings.title: Package defaults keep old finance parameters (annual 4%/2%); production profile does not carry the combined finance block
findings.intended: Combined spec: annual r=2%, delta=1.1% -> q=0.08243216, delta=0.04327930936, user_cost_rate=0.16571147.
findings.implemented: Defaults: 'P.q = (1.04 ** P.period_years) - 1.0' = 0.16985856 and 'P.delta = 1.0 - (1.0 - 0.02) ** P.period_years' = 0.07763184, user_cost_rate=0.28749 (1.73x). production_profile_overrides() contains no q/delta/eta_supply/H0 keys, so any tool applying only the profile silently runs the old regime (verified by probe). The combined drivers and combined-spec tools do pass the overrides explicitly.
findings.economic_consequence: Reproducibility hazard: diagnostics, plots, and audit tools run without fixed_model_overrides evaluate a materially different economy (gross period return 1.170 vs 1.082; rents 1.73x per unit p). Any post-overnight figure or moment produced through such a tool is not the estimated model.
findings.decisive_test: grep each tool used for reported artifacts for the q/delta override block; the probe in finance_supply_checks.json ('silent_default_drift_probe') already shows profile-only overrides yield q=0.169859, delta=0.077632.
findings.evidence: parameters.py:80-81: 'P.q = (1.04 ** P.period_years) - 1.0' / 'P.delta = 1.0 - (1.0 - 0.02) ** P.period_years'; production_profile.py:126-167 (no finance keys); run_overnight_combined.py:159-164 passes {'q': (1.0 + 0.02) ** period_years - 1.0, 'delta': 1.0 - (1.0 - 0.011) ** period_years, ...}

findings.title: Paper's incumbent no-delever LTV carve-out not implemented; uniform -phi*p*H floor applies to stayers
findings.intended: Paper: incumbent keeping the same house faces b' >= min{b, -phi P H_k} (not forced to delever after a price change).
findings.implemented: Owner Bellman lower bound is 'lo = bf' with bf = bmo = -phi*p*H for ALL active owners including stayers (kernels.py:922 'lo = bf'); verified numerically: min bp_pol per rung equals -phi*p*H exactly.
findings.economic_consequence: Vacuous in the estimated stationary equilibrium (the state can never begin below the floor at constant p), so no effect on the reported calibration; NOT vacuous in counterfactuals where p rises, where the code would force incumbents to delever while the paper says they are not.
findings.decisive_test: Solve a comparative-static with p raised 10% from the stationary solution and check whether any incumbent owner mass sits below the new -phi*p*H floor; if yes, code and paper diverge in policy experiments.
findings.evidence: kernels.py:920-924: 'lo = bf / if bg0 > lo: lo = bg0'; full_draft.tex: 'b'\ge \min\{b,-\phi P H_k\}' with 'An incumbent owner who keeps the same house is not forced to delever'

findings.title: tau_H aggregated linearly (0.01*4) while q and delta compound; spec-sheet delta4 constant slightly wrong
findings.intended: Uniform 4-year compounding of annual rates; lead's spec sheet states delta4=0.04328066.
findings.implemented: 'P.tau_H = 0.01 * P.period_years' = 0.04 (compounded would be 1-0.99^4=0.03940); exact delta4 = 1-(0.989)^4 = 0.04327930935900004, asserted in tools/compare_intergen_combined_specification.py:102, not 0.04328066.
findings.economic_consequence: ~0.4% overstatement of the user-cost rate from the tax component; negligible for fit but should be documented. The wrong delta4 constant in the audit spec sheet could propagate into cross-checks.
findings.evidence: parameters.py:82: 'P.tau_H = 0.01 * P.period_years'; compare_intergen_combined_specification.py:102: 'assert abs(converted["delta"] - 0.04327930935900004) <= 1e-14'

findings.title: Bequest base gross of liquidation costs vs paper 'after liquidation costs and taxes' (cross-flag to audit B)
findings.intended: Paper full_draft.tex:530: bequeathable wealth W^B is 'after liquidation costs and taxes'.
findings.implemented: 'hv = p_hat[i] * P.H_own[ten - 1] if ten > 0 else 0.0; ... Vbq[...] = bequest_utility_vec(b_grid + hv, nk, P)' — gross house value, no (1-psi), estate tax defaults 0, no final-period return on b.

Audit B owns the bequest block; recorded here because it is terminal-wealth accounting.

findings.intended: An approved, reproducible ACS target for aggregate mean occupied rooms of household heads 18-85 to discipline H0/supply.

The matching model-side moment mapping in calibration.py:1088-1092 is also uncommitted.

The value itself is verified (byte-exact vs the driver), but the calibration rests on an unreviewed, unapproved last-minute target per the repo's own status convention.

findings.title: Bootstrap SEs (available July 6, unused July 10) contradict the ad hoc weights by up to ~500x in relative terms
findings.intended: SMM weighting reflecting data precision (D10 optimal-weighting program); the SE/covariance file was produced specifically to unblock this.
findings.implemented: Overnight used CANDIDATE_REPLACEMENT_POST_AUDIT_V1_WEIGHTS (calibration.py:494-503) plus weight 6.0 on the new rooms target. Inverse-variance weights implied by the boot SEs: own_family_gap SE=0.00650 -> 23,650 vs used 45 (ratio 0.0019); old_age_own_rate SE=0.01259 -> 6,313 vs 160; old nh median SE=0.0886 -> 127 vs 0.8; old nh gap SE=0.682 -> 2.15 vs 2.0 (ratio 0.93). Spread of used/(1/SE^2) across moments ~500x; tfr/childless/aggregate-rooms have no SE at all.
findings.economic_consequence: The estimated point is a consistent but efficiency-arbitrary SMM estimate; loss values and cross-candidate rankings embed an arbitrary weighting; no standard errors, J-tests, or 'fit' significance statements are defensible from this loss. Notably the noisiest moment (old wealth gap, SE=68% of target) is near-optimally weighted while the four most precisely measured ownership moments are relatively downweighted 1-3 orders of magnitude.
findings.decisive_test: Recompute the objective at current_bound_best with W=diag(1/SE^2) (renormalized) and check whether the candidate ranking vs housing_relaxed_best flips; takes seconds from saved moments.
findings.evidence: moment_se.csv: `"own_family_gap",0.167662,...,0.00650257704353731,...`; `"old_parent_childless_nonhousing_wealth_to_income_gap_6575",1.00745,...,0.681912279121037`; calibration.py:494 `CANDIDATE_REPLACEMENT_POST_AUDIT_V1_WEIGHTS = {`; run_overnight_combined.py:158 `additional_weights={"aggregate_mean_occupied_rooms_18_85": 6.0}`.

findings.title: tfr=1.918 and childless_rate=0.188 have citation-level provenance only; no raw artifact or builder in repo
findings.intended: Reproducible completed-fertility targets with a checked-in source artifact.
findings.implemented: July 8 note pins both to CPS June 2024 Fertility Supplement, Census Historical Table H2, women 40-44 (CEB 1,918/1,000; 18.8% childless) — exact match, wave-consistent. But the SE harness gate (July 6) recorded `status=FAIL | source=source-unconfirmed` for both, the H2 table file is not checked in, and no builder exists; SEs are NA so these two moments cannot enter any variance-based weighting.
findings.economic_consequence: Reproducibility depends on an external published table; the childlessness target is strongly wave-dependent (0.150 in 2018 -> 0.188 in 2024), so the calibration is pinned to the 2024 wave by construction. CEB is per woman while the model unit is a household; the doubling convention (calibration.py:1094 `"tfr": 2.0 * household_parity`) is implemented, so the mapping is internally consistent.
findings.decisive_test: Check in the Census H2 XLSX (or a CPS June 2024 microdata extract reproducing 1.918/0.188) and add the two rows to a targets CSV with sample metadata.
findings.evidence: reproduction_log.txt: `key=tfr | target=1.91800000000 | reproduced=NA | ... | status=FAIL | source=source-unconfirmed`; TARGET_PROVENANCE_DRAFT.md: `Census Table H2, Women 40-44 ... 1,918 per 1,000 = 1.918 CEB/woman` and `None 18.8`.

findings.title: 'Aggregate' rooms target is a 42-metro MMS-sample mean, not a national ACS aggregate; room and ownership blocks use different head/structure universes
findings.intended: Per the audit brief: 'ACS aggregate mean occupied rooms for household heads 18-85' consistent across the target system.
findings.implemented: Sample is extract27 2012-2023 restricted to `met2013 > 0`, joined to MMS PUMA lookups, `in_mms_sample` (center/periphery, middle->center), heads defined as `pernum == 1` only, all structure types, literal ROOMS>0, HHWT (n=3,986,589). The four ownership targets (audit_ownership_targets.R:252-259) instead use `pernum == 1L & relate == 1L` heads plus `unitsstr %in% 3:10` and rooms>0 (DUE-housing). PSID targets are national all-individual samples.
findings.economic_consequence: Internally the five room targets are consistent (one builder pass; regenerated CSV reproduces the old prime30_55 constants exactly), so the model's one market is calibrated to the matched-metro universe throughout the room block — but a paper sentence calling 5.78 a US aggregate would be wrong, and the head/structure universe differs between the room block and the ownership block (mobile homes and non-relate-1 heads in one, not the other), a small but real cross-moment sample inconsistency.
findings.decisive_test: Recompute the aggregate mean with the DUE-housing filters (relate==1, unitsstr 3:10) and nationally (drop the MMS restriction) and report the deltas; grep the paper for the target's description.
findings.evidence: builder line 170: `filter(in_mms_sample, tenure %in% c("owner", "renter"))`; CSV: `n=3986589, weight=419883313, source="ACS/IPUMS extract27 household heads, 2012-2023, matched MMS metro sample, middle collapsed to center"`; audit_ownership_targets.R:252-259: `acs_head_all <- copy(acs[pernum == 1L & relate == 1L ...])` then `unitsstr %in% 3:10`.

findings.title: parent_3plus rooms target's only in-repo generator is the SE harness, not the dedicated target builder; young-wealth target double-serves as target and entry-wealth input
findings.intended: Every hard target emitted by a canonical builder; targets independent of model inputs.

(b) 0.17922556 is simultaneously the SMM target (calibration.py:157) and the mean of the external entrant-wealth distribution (calibration.py:33-56 nodes [-2.519,...,3.040], 'mean 0.17922556').
findings.economic_consequence: (a) Reproducibility hinges on an untracked harness file; if moment_standard_errors/ is never committed the gap reopens. (b) The young-wealth moment is partly mechanical: entrants are endowed with the very distribution whose mean is targeted, so its identifying content for saving-rate parameters is weaker than its weight (12) suggests — identification adjudicator should treat it as semi-external.
findings.decisive_test: (a) Add the moment to the canonical builder and diff against 0.36769955881. (b) Solve the model at the candidate with entry-wealth nodes shifted +/-20% and measure how much of the young-wealth moment is endowment vs behavior.

findings.title: Target-object audit doc (even the working-tree version) is stale on the old-age wealth denominator/statistic fixes
findings.intended: Docs matching the code the overnight ran.
findings.implemented: The doc still says the old gap 'Needs fix. Denominator and mean-of-ratios versus ratio-of-sums are not identical' and TARGET_MOMENT_OBJECTS (calibration.py:267-278) says 'needs-fix', but solver.py:4895-4966 now computes both the median level and the parent-childless gap from cell ratios `bg[positive] / yj` with `yj = annual_gross_income_at_state(P, i, jj, 1.0)` (annual gross) and gap = difference of weighted_mean_from_cells (mean-of-ratios) — matching the PSID data construction (weighted mean/median of NETWORTH2R/INCFAMR, income>1000).
findings.economic_consequence: No estimation error — the code is right; but the audit trail says the opposite, which will mislead reviewers and future sessions into re-fixing or distrusting a correct moment. Residual genuine caveat: yj is evaluated at z=1.0, ignoring income dispersion in the ratio denominator for the partially-working 65-66 model period.
findings.decisive_test: One-line unit check: recompute old_nonhousing_wealth_to_income_median_6575 from a saved solution by hand with annual income and compare to sol attribute; then update the ledger entries.

findings.title: Old-age parent-childless wealth gap target is statistically near-uninformative (bootstrap SE = 68% of target) with untrimmed negative-wealth ratios
findings.intended: A moment identifying the bequest/child-saving margin (theta_n block).
findings.implemented: Gap = parent minus childless weighted MEAN of individual NETWORTH2R/INCFAMR ratios, income>1000 only guard, negatives and extreme ratios untrimmed; family-cluster bootstrap SE 0.6819 on target 1.00745 (moment_se.csv), i.e. the 95% CI spans roughly [-0.33, 2.34].
findings.economic_consequence: Any parameter identified primarily off this moment (the theta_n bequest-by-children block — already flagged unidentified in the July 8 verdicts) is effectively unconstrained by data; hitting or missing this target is statistically meaningless, and its weight (2.0) coincidentally near-optimal only because the moment is so noisy.
findings.decisive_test: Report the gap with 1%/99% ratio winsorization and as a median gap from the same builder; if the point estimate moves by more than the model-data gap, the moment cannot discipline anything.
findings.evidence: moment_se.csv: `"old_parent_childless_nonhousing_wealth_to_income_gap_6575",1.00745,1.00744952426822,...,0.681912279121037,11449,4263,"PSID old-age wealth"`; builder lines 105-107: `nonhousing_nw_to_income = if_else(income > 1000, nonhousing_nw / income, NA_real_)` with no trimming.

findings.title: Income-process dispersion reverse-engineered from the old ad hoc 5-point grid; persistent income risk 2-3.5x below standard calibrations
findings.intended: A defensible annual AR(1) in log productivity (rho_annual=0.9602, innovation sd 0.0645), discretized by Rouwenhorst, as the combined spec's income-risk primitive.
findings.implemented: The annual parameters are exactly the old ad hoc grid re-expressed: rho_annual == 0.85**(1/4) (diff 0.0) where 0.85 is DEFAULT_RHO_Z of the old stay/redraw process, and the stationary log variance equals the old grid's weighted log variance 0.0533671307381197 to 3e-17 (snapshot tmp/rouwenhorst_matched_nb120_4h_SOURCE_SNAPSHOT.txt:7-16 states this matching explicitly). Stationary log sd 0.231 vs the repo's own Sommer-Sullivan reference 0.459 (rho=0.90, sd=0.20 at local_panel.py:44-45) and literature stationary log sd ~0.46-0.80 (Floden-Linde, STY, KMP; quoted from memory, verify before citing). Level sd rises slightly 0.219->0.232 because matching was in logs.
findings.economic_consequence: Persistent income risk is far below standard values, muting precautionary saving, wealth dispersion, and income-gradient mechanisms. Five active targets are wealth-to-income or income-conditioned moments; parameters absorbing missing income risk (beta, theta0/theta_n, chi) are estimated against an understated risk process, and policy counterfactuals inherit it. The paper discloses the matching (draft lines 444-449, 955-956) but the matched object never had an empirical source, so the calibration is not defensible as stated.
findings.decisive_test: Fixed-theta A/B at the current_bound_best theta: rerun via tools/compare_intergen_income_processes.py-style overrides with the Sommer-Sullivan process (rho=0.90, sd=0.20) vs the matched process at Nb=120 and compare the full target-fit table, especially the wealth-to-income moments and own_rate gradients.
findings.evidence: local_panel.py:42 "DEFAULT_RHO_Z = 0.85"; check script: rho_annual - 0.85**0.25 = 0.0; old grid log var 0.0533671307381197 vs new process log var 0.05336713073811974; snapshot: "matched to the current draft income process's four-year persistence and unconditional log variance ... unconditional log variance: 0.0533671307381197"

findings.title: Eight checked-in tools and the CLI silently evaluate combined-spec candidates under the OLD income process
findings.intended: Any evaluation, policy experiment, or sensitivity run on the July-10 candidate must use the matched Rouwenhorst process plus the combined fixed spec (q, delta, eta, bequest normalization).
findings.implemented: These tools call income_process_overrides(income_states) with the default process="current", i.e., the old [0.6..1.4] grid with stay/redraw Pi_z at rho 0.85, and none applies the combined fixed spec. cli.py's polish/global-de/panel default to --income-process current, and if "rouwenhorst" is selected bare, defaults rho=0.90 sd=0.20 (Sommer-Sullivan, 4x the matched dispersion). production_profile_overrides() still ships the old grid (production_profile.py:136-141) and validate_production_profile never checks the income process; the combined spec survives only because run_local_panel_case merges {base, **extra, **income, **theta} (local_panel.py:848-853). The matched constants exist in source only in run_intergen_combined_recalibration.py, compare_intergen_bequest_normalization.py, and build_intergen_mechanics_packet.py (behind --combined-corrected-spec).
findings.economic_consequence: Post-overnight policy numbers, sensitivity Jacobians, or pathology audits produced by these tools on the new candidate would be internally inconsistent with the estimated model (different income risk, income timing, and old q/delta/eta/bequest spec) while appearing profile-validated. No contaminated artifact was identified in this audit, but the hazard is one command away during advisor week.
findings.decisive_test: Grep any post-July-10 output JSON claiming the combined candidate for income_process_fingerprint and compare to 5b6875a240e82073e515a291469e2d259c35bb01ca63cabfb10b639700db5daf; alternatively run run_intergen_policy_poc.py on the candidate and confirm its baseline loss does NOT reproduce 14.780.
findings.evidence: run_intergen_policy_poc.py:195 "**income_process_overrides(income_states),"; cli.py:137-139 "--income-process ... default='current'", "--income-annual-rho ... default=0.90", "--income-innovation-sd ... default=0.20"; production_profile.py:136-138 "'z_grid': PRODUCTION_Z_GRID.copy(), 'z_weights': PRODUCTION_Z_WEIGHTS.copy(), 'income_shock_persistence': PRODUCTION_INCOME_PERSISTENCE"

findings.title: Legacy mean-income denominator uses entry-age income; overwritten in the markov path but wrong for any non-markov diagnostic
findings.intended: wealth_to_income and liquid_wealth_to_income denominators should use mean worker income over the age profile (and z).
findings.implemented: compute_statistics sets mean_income from age-0 income only: ti = sum(P.income[i, 0] * worker_mass). With the current profile, entry income is 0.7206 of the working mean, overstating those ratios by ~1.39x. compute_markov_statistics recomputes mean_income, wealth_to_income, liquid_wealth_to_income, and the young_* moments with the full age-and-z-weighted income (solver.py:4523-4547), so the combined-spec run and all active targets are unaffected.
findings.economic_consequence: Only legacy single-type or permanent-type diagnostics inherit a ~39% overstated wealth-to-income ratio; production markov results are clean. Was exactly correct only under the retired legacy_entry_income_peak=True convention, which is why it survived.
findings.decisive_test: Solve a tiny grid with use_income_types=False and compare stats.mean_income to the age-profile-weighted mean; they differ by the factor work_mean/profile[0] = 1/0.7206.
findings.evidence: solver.py:4717 "ti = sum(P.income[i, 0] * stats.worker_mass_by_loc[i] for i in range(I))" vs solver.py:4543 (markov override) "stats.mean_income = worker_income / max(worker_mass, 1e-12)"

findings.title: compare_intergen_income_processes.py documents the Sommer-Sullivan parameters, not the process that was actually run
findings.intended: The named comparison tool should recover the provenance of the production income process.
findings.implemented: The tool hard-codes ANNUAL_RHO=0.90, ANNUAL_INNOVATION_SD=0.20 ("Sommer--Sullivan annual specification") for its rouwenhorst cases; the matched values 0.9602/0.0645 actually used in the overnight appear nowhere in it. The matching procedure itself (persistence + log-variance match to the old grid) is documented only in tmp snapshot text files and hard-coded constants in three other tools.
findings.economic_consequence: A reader tracing the production income process through the obvious tool infers a process with 4x the calibrated dispersion; reproduction and referee-facing documentation of the actual matching procedure depend on tmp files that are not source-controlled provenance.
findings.decisive_test: Run the tool with --construction-only and compare its rouwenhorst5 unconditional_log_variance (0.2105) to the run snapshot's 0.05337.
findings.evidence: compare_intergen_income_processes.py:54-55 "ANNUAL_RHO = 0.90\nANNUAL_INNOVATION_SD = 0.20"; docstring lines 4-7 "start from the Sommer--Sullivan annual specification rho=0.90 ... 0.20"
