verdict_entries.severity_adjustment: Keep MAJOR, but re-scope: the finding is a reproducibility/version-control defect, not a threat to the estimated results (the overnight ran the dirty tree with the intended Rouwenhorst process; exact reproduction already verified). Two corrections to the narrative: (1) reproduction from HEAD fails with an immediate TypeError (the Rouwenhorst helper signature and run_local_polish kwargs do not exist at HEAD), i.e., loud, not silent; (2) the silent-divergence risk is a latent hybrid-tree/partial-commit hazard, made dangerous by the intent-based income fingerprint at local_panel.py:116-119 and quantitatively large if triggered (+6.30 loss = +29% at the incumbent theta at Nb=40, with a strict-convergence flip and a sign-flipping room-gap moment). If the lead's severity scale reserves MAJOR for effects on reported results, this downgrades to MINOR (repro process) plus a MAJOR-worthy action item: commit the full Rouwenhorst block including the one-line merge order, and add an effective-parameter fingerprint assertion.

verdict_entries.reasoning:
The code reading is accurate: both tools are hard-wired to the dt_cp two-location strand and can express no element of the combined intergen spec. regen_lifecycle_compare_notitle.py imports build_setup/solve_record from make_full_dispersion_diagnostic_note, whose build_setup (line 237) calls build_direct_calibration_setup("benchmark", geo_weight=250.0, population_closure="outside_option_benchmark_normalized", ...) and whose solve_record injects two-location objects (lines 268-269) and solves via dt_cp_model.solver.run_model_cp_dt (line 274). run_policy_counterfactuals_from_record.py does the same through its own build_setup (lines 250-251) and solve path (lines 424-425). A grep of both scripts for rouwenhorst/normalize_bequest returns zero hits (the eta_supply/H0 hits in run_policy are the dt_cp two-location P.H0[1]/center objects, not the intergen scalar H0). regen defaults --hR-max to 8.0 (line 19) versus the production profile's renter cap 6.

However, the claimed MAJOR severity does not survive a materiality test, for three quantified reasons. (1) Zero effect on any reported number: the finding itself concedes these tools did NOT produce the current paper artifacts (frozen intergen scripts and the tmp phase9b driver did). The 14.780 and 13.903 candidate losses, the target-fit tables, and the policy results are all computed through intergen_housing_fertility.local_panel; nothing in the audited object touches these two files. (2) The feared misuse — pointing them at a combined-spec record and silently getting a different model — is mechanically impossible with the actual record schema. The intergen record (output/model/combined_recalibration/overnight_20260710_report/current_bound_best.json) stores theta as a dict of 14 intergen names (H0, alpha_cons, beta, ..., no E_C, no r_bar_C). Both scripts execute np.asarray(record["theta"], dtype=float) as essentially their first act on the record (solve_record line 259; run_policy line 100), and I ran this against the real record in the model venv: it raises TypeError ("float() argument must be a string or a real number, not 'dict'") immediately. Even if theta were an array, theta_dict["E_C"] would KeyError at lines 268/424. The failure mode is a crash at record load, not a silently wrong figure or policy table. (3) run_policy has an additional guard: it re-solves the baseline and compares moments to record["moments"] (lines 117-128), writing baseline_resolve_check.csv and warning (default --baseline-mismatch warn, line 203) on divergence — so even a hand-adapted record would flag itself.

What remains is a real but small hazard: two in-repo tools with paper-figure-shaped names belong to the superseded dt_cp strand, while the intergen paper's actual regeneration path lives in tmp/ and a frozen candidate directory. That is a tool-hygiene / discoverability gap (arguably part of a separate provenance/staleness finding), not something that materially changes interpretation, fit, policy, or reproducibility of the estimated model. Smallest decisive fix: rename/relocate or docstring-tag both tools as dt_cp-only, and add (or point to) an intergen-native regeneration entry point in code/model/tools or the intergen README.
verdict_entries.severity_adjustment: Downgrade MAJOR to MINOR. The finding's own text concedes the tools produced none of the current paper artifacts, so no estimated result, candidate loss, figure, or policy number is affected. The escalation rationale ("silently solves a different model if pointed at an intergen record") is empirically false: the actual combined-spec record format (theta as name-keyed dict, no E_C/r_bar_C) makes both scripts crash with TypeError at record load (verified by execution against current_bound_best.json), and run_policy additionally self-checks the re-solved baseline against record moments. The residual risk is discoverability confusion for future sessions — a documentation/naming issue, which is MINOR under the audit's rubric. If the lead wants to preserve a MAJOR somewhere, the better target is the flip-side provenance issue (the paper's true regeneration path living outside the repo's discoverable tools), which is a distinct finding.

verdict_entries.reasoning:
All mechanical facts in T3 verify exactly, and a new within-spec experiment I ran confirms the economic substance while refining the mechanism.

VERIFIED FACTS: (1) Target 1.00744952 at calibration.py:96 and weight 2.0 at calibration.py:420; gap moment defined as parent-minus-childless old nonhousing wealth/income at solver.py:4961. (2) Contributions: 0.119051/14.780 = 0.805% and 0.115045/13.903 = 0.827% (target_audit recompute, independently reproduced by the targets auditor and re-checked by me against the printed table). (3) theta_n = 0.7679202774006243 (current) vs 1.109009045400624 (housing_relaxed) — 44.4% apart — with theta0 bit-identical (0.1318301350511569) in both; my full parameter diff shows theta_n is by far the largest relative move (next is h_bar_n at 16.1%). (4) Gap moment 1.2514 vs 1.2473 across candidates (0.33% apart) while theta_n moves 44%. (5) June 18 cos(psi_child,theta_n)=0.975, cos(kappa_fert,theta_n)=-0.946 confirmed at docs/model/intergen_sensitivity_jacobian_audit_20260618.md:126-127; July 8 D11 evidence is even stronger than T3 quotes: near-null Jacobian direction was theta_n ALONE (loading +0.998, max scaled derivative 0.015 on any moment) — though at a theta0≈0.0015 point, so it does not mechanically carry to the new interior-theta0 spec.

NEW DECISIVE EVIDENCE (my Nb=40 controlled experiment, output/model/full_audit_20260711/verify_T3/): swapping ONLY theta_n 0.768→1.109 at current_bound_best under the exact overnight evaluation path (both runs strict, residuals ~3.9e-5): loss 21.390→18.973. Refinement 1: theta_n is NOT loss-inert under this spec (the literal D11 null-direction claim does not carry over once theta0 is interior). Refinement 2 — and this is what sustains the finding: the designed identifying moment moved AWAY from target (gap 1.555→1.718, contribution +0.41), while the loss change was driven by old_nonhousing_wealth_median (-1.33), rooms contrasts (-0.82/-0.77/-0.51), young liquid wealth (+0.68), TFR (+0.113) and childless rate (-2.9pp) — every one a channel shared with beta/theta0/psi_child/kappa_fert/h_bar_n. So theta_n is disciplined only through collinear incidental channels, not by its designed weight-2 moment; between the two candidates those channels are exactly re-compensated by 4-16% moves in eight other parameters, leaving the gap moment within 0.3%. This is set identification along a ridge, empirically demonstrated. Corroborating: SEARCH_INTEGRITY.md:121-142 shows both candidates are pattern-polish descendants of ONE wave-1 seed — two local descents from the same point diverged 44% in theta_n at near-equal loss, the signature of a flat direction. Also, at Nb=40 the loss IMPROVES when theta_n rises within the production box, i.e., the coordinate is not even locally pinned at the reported point on a coarse grid (grid caveat: Nb=40 baseline loss 21.39 vs 14.78 at Nb=120, so magnitudes are indicative, direction and moment-composition are the reliable content).

MATERIALITY: the bequest tilt is the strand's headline structural object. Across the two candidates the n=2 bequest weight theta0(1+2*theta_n) is 0.334 vs 0.424 (27% apart); the parent-vs-childless marginal tilt differs 44%. Any intergenerational/bequest counterfactual inherits at least this unresolved range while the 15-moment system cannot distinguish the economies. Interaction with BEQUEST_AUDIT F4 (theta1=0.01 saturation) explains WHY the designed identification fails: bequest utility is nearly flat in wealth over most of its range, so theta_n acts largely as a family-size-dependent terminal utility level — it moves fertility and broad old-wealth moments, not specifically the parent-childless nonhousing saving gap.

CAVEATS THAT DO NOT OVERTURN: (a) the 13.90 candidate lives in the relaxed box (h_bar_0=0.921<1.0, chi=1.2006>1.15), so the pair is not literally two admissible points of the production spec; but my within-box experiment and the single-seed lineage independently establish the flat/compensable direction inside the production box. (b) 14.78 vs 13.90 is 6% apart, not identical; still far inside the spread that a 44% structural-parameter move should produce if identified. (c) "only moment that separates theta_n" is slightly overstated as written — own_family_gap and the parent-3plus rooms contrast also condition on family status — but the gap moment is the only designed parent-childless WEALTH contrast, and my experiment shows even it is dominated (weight 2, ~0.8% of loss) by incidental channels weighted 6-25.

The proposed fix-and-repolish test remains the right formal test; my evidence already makes its outcome highly predictable (comparable losses at both theta_n values). This is not a code bug and was a documented open item (D11 'must resolve in Phase 3'), so it does not invalidate the candidates' fit — hence MAJOR, not FATAL.
verdict_entries.severity_adjustment: Keep MAJOR, with scope precision: MAJOR for interpretation and for any intergenerational/bequest counterfactual or paper claim that uses theta_n (or decompositions of the parent-childless saving wedge), and for reporting discipline — under the repo's own identification rules the calibration should be reported as effectively 13-of-14 identified or theta_n fixed externally. NOT major for the reported fit, the other 13 parameters, or reproducibility, which are unaffected. Do not upgrade to FATAL: theta_n weakness does not contaminate the loss, the moment fits, or non-bequest results, and it was a pre-documented open item the spec failed to resolve rather than a defect introduced by the last-minute changes.

verdict_entries.reasoning:
The implemented behavior is exactly as the finding describes, verified by direct code trace plus a numeric underflow check.

(1) Sentinel origin. Infeasible birth/tenure options are hard-coded to -1e10, not merely penalized: eval_renter sets the conditional renter value to -1e10 when cash-on-hand cannot cover the child-cost floor (solver.py:2840 `f[surplus <= 1e-10] = -1e10`), eval_owner analogously (solver.py:2871), and the tenure-choice stage masks purchase options failing the down-payment/borrowing constraints (solver.py:2255, 2266 `Vrs[inf_m, nn, cs] = -1e10`; kernel path lines 2727/2731/2742 mirror this).

(2) Propagation. The tenure logsumexp (solver.py:2270) and location logit (solver.py:2299) preserve ~-1e10 when all options for a given (n>=1, cs=1) column are infeasible, so Vfa[n>=1] (built at solver.py:2306-2308 from VI at cs=1) is ~-1e10 while Vfa[n=0] is finite.

(3) Exact zero. The fertility logit (solver.py:2309-2311 `lf = Vfa / P.kappa_fert; ls, pr = logsumexp(lf, axis=3)`) uses utils.logsumexp (utils.py:194-199, `probs = np.exp(a - ls)`). With kappa_fert = 1.8906304 (candidate value, repro/current_repro_exact.json:214), a - ls ≈ -5.29e9, and np.exp underflows to exactly 0.0. I reproduced this with the venv python: probs = [1,0,0,0], p_birth == 0.0 exactly. Because float64 exp underflows only below ~-745, an exact-zero birth probability requires a value gap > kappa*745 ≈ 1410 utils — in practice only the -1e10 sentinel produces this, so exact zeros are a clean marker of full infeasibility of the birth option across all tenures and destinations. For any finite value gap the logit gives strictly positive probability, so the corner is genuinely a feasibility cliff, not a smoothed tail.

(4) Magnitudes. The quoted artifact numbers are real and internally consistent: diagnostics/current_bound/numeric_checks.json fertility_probs gives p_birth_zero_cell_share = 0.08868 (2554/28800 cells), childless_fertile_mass = 0.24338, childless_mass_at_p_birth_zero = 0.09798, share = 0.40256; probe_checks.json p_birth_zero_decomposition gives invalid_node_mass = 0.0, valid_node_mass = 0.09798, renter tenure 0.09185, ages concentrated at j=0-2 (0.0357+0.0285+0.0128), mean_wealth_pbirth0 = 0.00018 vs 0.12324 for all childless fertile. 0.09798/0.24338 = 0.4026 checks out. I did not re-solve the model to recompute the mass weights independently (battery constraint), but the decomposition sits on valid distribution nodes per the probe, and the lead already reproduced this candidate exactly, so the fert_probs/KFE objects behind these numbers are the audited ones.

Net: the finding's factual content is correct — 40% of childless fertile-window mass sits at p_birth identically zero, driven by the child-cost/down-payment feasibility sentinel, concentrated on near-zero-wealth young renters, and kappa_fert smoothing is inoperative there.

verdict_entries.severity_adjustment: Keep MAJOR for the economic-interpretation consequence, but the lead should relabel the finding's framing: this is NOT an intended-vs-implemented code discrepancy. Assigning probability zero to budget-infeasible birth options is the correct implementation of a Gumbel choice over the feasible set; the -1e10 sentinel is a deliberate feasibility encoding, and the logit handles it exactly as designed. What the finding correctly establishes is a verified model property with material consequences: (a) housing-cost-to-fertility counterfactuals will move mass across a hard feasibility boundary (kinked, potentially jumpy comparative statics at coarse Nb), and (b) kappa_fert is identified only off the ~60% of childless mass with interior probabilities. One caveat on the proposed decisive test: a ±1% price perturbation at Nb=120 will confound the feasibility-cliff jump with b-grid discreteness (the mass sits at the bottom grid nodes, mean wealth 2e-4), so any jump found there needs a grid-refinement check before being attributed to the cliff.

verdict_entries.reasoning: The finding accurately characterizes the code. (1) Entry injection has no feasibility screen: solver.py:3713-3715 computes grid weights from PSID wealth/income ratios times entrant income (solver.py:302-305, pure linear interpolation onto b_grid via _linear_grid_weights_for_points) and adds mass directly to renter j=0 nodes; nothing checks whether those nodes are Bellman-feasible. (2) Infeasible renter nodes carry V=-1e10 (solver.py:2631) and placeholder policies: c = c_bar_0 + c_min, hR = h_bar_0 + 0.01 (solver.py:2639-2641), while the stored savings policy bd is whatever the unmasked search returned (solver.py:2643). (3) The KFE structurally cannot exclude this mass: forward_distribution_markov_income (solver.py:3683-3696) does not receive V at all, so injected mass on sentinel nodes evolves under placeholder policies and enters all distribution-based moments. (4) The magnitudes are reproduced from independent artifacts: probe script defines invalid = V <= -1e9 against the -1e10 sentinel (audit_diag_packet.py:42) on a fixed-price PE re-solve of the exact candidate theta (c_bar_0 = 1.28 confirmed in repro/current_repro_exact.json); probe_checks.json gives total populated-invalid mass 0.017195 = 1.72% of total mass (sum g = 1), by_age[0] = 0.011986, and with numeric_checks.json age_mass[0] = 0.0588235 the cohort share is 0.011986/0.0588235 = 20.38% ~ 20.4%. Invalid mass is almost entirely renters (0.017115 of 0.017195), wealth range [-2.907, 2.953]. Additionally, locate_invalid_j0.json shows the infeasible entrant nodes have degenerate uniform fert_probs [1/3,1/3,1/3] (a logit over all-sentinel values), so these households' births are pure noise — the by_parity split (0.0040/0.0053/0.0079) confirms most invalid mass has been assigned children this way, which contaminates young fertility moments beyond what the finding states. Attempted refutation angles all fail: there is no downstream mass reallocation, no moment-side V mask, and no renormalization that would neutralize the injected infeasible mass. One clause is moot rather than wrong: with I=1 in the production profile, 'poisons entry values' has no location-entry bite for this spec (entry_by_loc trivial), though the entry-value computation (solver.py:349-364) does average over the same nodes including V=-1e10. Severity MAJOR is appropriate: 20.4% of each entering cohort and 1.72% of steady-state mass, concentrated in young renters, directly feeds own_rate_2534, young liquid-wealth, rooms, and young fertility moments with non-optimizing placeholder behavior; not FATAL because 98.3% of mass is on valid nodes and the invalid mass decays fast (0.0032 at j=1, 0.00095 at j=2).

verdict_entries.severity_adjustment: MAJOR is correct. Two refinements: (a) the finding slightly understates the contamination channel — infeasible entrant nodes get degenerate uniform fertility probabilities (1/3 each over sentinel values), so ~2/3 of the deepest-infeasible entrant mass is assigned immediate births by pure Gumbel noise (locate_invalid_j0.json), directly touching TFR/childlessness at young ages, not only housing/wealth moments; (b) the "poisons entry values" clause is moot for this production spec because I=1 makes the location-entry margin trivial, though the code path (solver.py:349-364) does include V=-1e10 nodes as claimed.

verdict_entries.reasoning:
INC-2 is CONFIRMED at MAJOR, and the contamination magnitude, if any of these tools is run on the July-10 candidate, is first-order, not second-order. I ran the decisive test the finding proposed, extended to three arms (script: output/model/full_audit_20260711/scripts/verify_inc2_spec_gap.py; results: output/model/full_audit_20260711/spec_audit/inc2_severity_spec_gap_nb40.json). Same candidate theta (current_bound_best, H0=9.9997 etc.), same 15-moment target system, Nb=40, max_iter_eq=10: (A) correct combined spec -> loss 21.390, p_eq=0.6907, strict converged; (B) the checked-in tool path (production profile + income_process_overrides default "current", no q/delta/eta/bequest fixes) -> loss 48.289 (2.3x), p_eq=0.3763, strict=False (residual 5.6e-4 > tol 1e-4); (C) cli.py bare --income-process rouwenhorst defaults -> loss 152.731 (7.1x), p_eq=0.3748. The tool-path economy clears at a house price 46 percent below the estimated model's, own_rate jumps 0.585->0.754, own_rate_2534 0.166->0.401, TFR 1.845->2.111, old nonhousing wealth/income median 0.883->5.699, mean rooms 5.56->6.77. Any policy delta or Jacobian computed off that baseline describes a different economy, so the "baseline would not reproduce 14.780" prediction is verified with room to spare.

Code reading verified: all eight tools call income_process_overrides with default process="current" (rg hits at run_intergen_policy_poc.py:195, audit_intergen_final_best_pathologies.py:148, audit_intergen_solver_accuracy.py:208, audit_intergen_mechanism_grid.py:195, audit_intergen_sensitivity_jacobian.py:318, audit_intergen_parent_credit_margin.py:256, run_intergen_code_repair_comparison.py:74, intergen_surrogate_calibration/validate_proposals.py:42); run_intergen_policy_poc.py additionally never applies production_profile_overrides at all (its merge at lines 193-198 is base_overrides + income + theta + case). All three CLI subcommands default --income-process current (cli.py:91, 111, 137). validate_production_profile (production_profile.py:194-224) checks only J/Nb/n_house/income_states-count/target_set/max_iter_eq — it cannot detect income-process content or the fixed spec, confirming "appears profile-validated". The matched constants exist in source only in the three files the finding names (rg for 0.9601845894041878 hits run_intergen_combined_recalibration.py, build_intergen_mechanics_packet.py, compare_intergen_bequest_normalization.py, plus tmp/overnight_combined_20260710/run_overnight_combined.py).

Why MAJOR and not FATAL or MINOR: not FATAL because no contaminated artifact exists — I scanned all post-July-10 outputs; the only policy battery (output/model/intergen_repair_full13_nb120_best6942_policy_battery_20260710) is the OLD 6.942 candidate evaluated under its own consistent old spec, which is self-consistent, not contamination; the 14.780/13.903 candidates and the lead's reproduction used the correct tmp-driver environment and are unaffected. Not MINOR because this is a genuine reproducibility defect of the deliverable today: the estimated model's specification is not represented anywhere in checked-in production tooling, every standard post-estimation entry point silently evaluates a ~46-percent-lower-price economy while passing profile validation, and the live plan's explicit next step (CALIBRATION_STATUS.md:126 "recalibration at 120 -> verify at 240 -> policy tables") routes through exactly these tools during advisor week. Any artifact actually produced through these paths would itself be FATAL-grade wrong.

One attribution correction the lead should note: the finding headlines the "OLD income process", but the income-process component alone is nearly immaterial by construction — the matched Rouwenhorst was fit to the old process (annualized persistence identical, 0.85^0.25 = 0.96018 = MATCHED_ANNUAL_RHO to 5 decimals; stationary level dispersion 0.219 old vs approx 0.234 matched, a 7 percent gap). The dominant contamination in arm B is the missing combined fixed spec: per-period q 0.1699 vs 0.0824, delta 0.0776 vs 0.0433 (q+delta user-cost core 0.2475 vs 0.1257, ratio 1.97), eta_supply 1.0 vs 1.75, normalize_bequest_utility False vs True. Also, the finding's "4x the matched dispersion" for bare rouwenhorst defaults overstates slightly: innovation sd 0.20 vs 0.06454 is 3.1x, stationary log-sd 0.459 vs 0.231 is 2.0x — though arm C's 7.1x loss confirms it is the worst path regardless. Smallest fix implied: add the combined spec (income constants + q/delta/eta/bequest) to production_profile.py or a named spec function, and make validate_production_profile check an income/finance fingerprint before any post-estimation tool run.
verdict_entries.severity_adjustment: Keep MAJOR, with two refinements: (1) reframe the headline from "old income process" to "missing combined fixed spec plus old income process" — the fixed spec (q, delta, eta_supply, bequest normalization) drives most of the 21.4->48.3 loss blowout and the 0.69->0.38 price gap, since the matched Rouwenhorst was designed to replicate the old process's persistence and dispersion; (2) classify it as a latent reproducibility hazard: existing reported results are clean, but any future artifact produced through these eight tools or the CLI defaults on the combined candidate would be FATAL-grade wrong while passing profile validation, and policy tables through run_intergen_policy_poc.py are the stated next step.

verdict_entries.reasoning:
The facts are correct and the mechanics check out end-to-end, but the severity is MAJOR only in a narrowed sense (policy-counterfactual interpretation and the kappa_fert narrative), not for the estimated candidates, the fit, or reproducibility, and it is NOT a code defect.

(1) Mechanics verified. The fertility stage builds Vfa from post-location values (solver.py:2306-2308) and applies a plain Gumbel logit (solver.py:2310 "ls, pr = logsumexp(lf, axis=3)" with lf = Vfa/P.kappa_fert). The logsumexp implementation (utils.py:194-199, "probs = np.exp(a - ls)") gives exactly 0.0 probability when an option carries the -1e10 sentinel: with kappa_fert=1.89 the exponent is about -5.3e9, which underflows to exact zero in float64. So p_birth = 0 exactly wherever all n>=1 options are -1e10 and n=0 is finite. The masses quoted match the saved evidence bit-for-bit: numeric_checks.json p_birth_zero_cell_share=0.08868 (2554/28800 cells), childless_mass_share_at_p_birth_zero=0.40256 (0.09798/0.24338); probe_checks.json invalid_node_mass=0.0, by_tenure renter=0.09185 of 0.09798, by_age concentrated at j=0-2 (0.0357+0.0285+0.0128=0.077 of 0.098, exactly zero from j=7), mean_wealth_pbirth0=0.00018 vs 0.12324.

(2) It is not a bug, and "intended: interior probabilities" overstates the intent. The -1e10 marks genuine budget infeasibility: eval_renter sets "f[surplus <= 1e-10] = -1e10" (solver.py:2840) when resources cannot cover the child minimums c_bar = c_bar_0+c_bar_n*nk and h_bar = h_bar_0+h_bar_jump+h_bar_n*nk (solver.py:1931-1935) at any savings choice; eval_owner analogously at solver.py:2871; the tenure-stage down-payment/borrowing screen at solver.py:2265-2266 ("inf_m = (b_grid < dpc) | (bar < bmn)"). Under additive Gumbel taste shocks over a feasible choice set, zero probability on a budget-infeasible alternative is the mathematically correct object — a taste shock cannot finance c<=0. Implemented = defensible math. The corner (poor young households cannot afford a child yet) is arguably the paper's affordability mechanism itself, not a distortion of it.

(3) No effect on the reported candidates. The 14.780 loss was reproduced exactly by the lead with this corner present; the corner is a property of the estimated model at both candidates, and the moments were computed with it. Nothing about 14.780/13.903, the target-fit table, or reproducibility changes. Not FATAL by any reading.

(4) Where the MAJOR severity legitimately bites — two places, both interpretive:
(a) kappa_fert interpretation/identification: kappa_fert governs only the interior 60% (mass 0.145 of 0.243), and the extensive-margin fertility moments load partly on c_bar_n/h_bar_jump/h_bar_n through the feasibility boundary rather than on kappa_fert. That substitutability is a real identification caveat, though the corner empties with age (0.0031 by j=5-6, exactly 0 at j>=7: households age/save out of it), so lifecycle timing moments still discipline the smoothing parameter.
(b) Policy counterfactuals: for 40% of the childless pool the individual response to a housing-cost policy is a 0->interior jump at the feasibility boundary. Because wealth lives on a discrete grid (Nb=120, b-step ~0.353) and the corner mass is stacked at the very bottom (mean wealth 0.0002), the aggregate fertility response to policy size is a staircase whose step location depends on grid placement. Order of magnitude: 0.098 of population mass sits at the corner; if a policy renders the boundary slack and these households adopt poor-interior birth probabilities (~0.1-0.3 per 4-year period), the first-birth flow moves by roughly 0.01-0.03 of population mass per period — the policy fertility elasticity is dominated by the feasibility margin, not by the logit. This materially conditions how any fertility policy number from this model should be read. Note this interacts with, and is partly subsumed by, the already-standing Nb=60->240 non-convergence finding (dp overstated 5-12%): grid sensitivity of policy numbers was already flagged; DIAG-3 identifies the specific mechanism (boundary-crossing mass) behind it for fertility.

(5) Adjacent, worse pathology the finding should not be conflated with: locate_invalid_j0.json shows populated nodes where even V(n=0) is -1e10 yet fert_probs = [0.3333, 0.3333, 0.3333] (uniform over ALL-infeasible options, mass 0.0172 per probe_checks populated_invalid). That uniform-1/3 behavior on fully-invalid populated nodes is a genuine smoothing artifact (logsumexp of equal sentinels), distinct from DIAG-3's correct p=0, and belongs to whichever finding covers populated-invalid mass.

(6) Proposed decisive test: the ±1% price perturbation at fixed theta is the right test for the staircase claim but requires Nb=120 solves (~2x67s each) to be decisive — a tiny-grid version would have different corner geometry and would not speak to the candidate. I did not run it (battery constraint, and the verdict is determinable from the saved solution evidence plus code mechanics). It should be queued as a follow-up if policy numbers from this calibration are to be quoted.

Evidence files read: output/model/full_audit_20260711/diagnostics/current_bound/numeric_checks.json (p_birth_zero_cell_share=0.08868, childless_mass_share_at_p_birth_zero=0.40256, b-grid [-12,30] Nb=120); probe_checks.json (valid_node_mass=0.09798, invalid_node_mass=0.0, by_tenure/by_age/by_z decompositions, mean_wealth_pbirth0=0.00018, populated_invalid total_mass=0.01719); locate_invalid_j0.json (fert_probs=[0.3333,...] on V=-1e10 populated rows).
verdict_entries.severity_adjustment: Keep MAJOR but narrow its scope and rewrite the headline. It is not a code defect (exact-zero probability on budget-infeasible alternatives is correct Gumbel discrete-choice math over the feasible set), it does not touch the reproduced 14.780/13.903 losses, the target fit, or reproducibility, and the corner is largely the paper's own affordability mechanism. MAJOR applies specifically to (i) interpretation of policy fertility counterfactuals — responses are dominated by boundary-crossing mass (0.098 of population at the corner; potential first-birth flow jumps of order 0.01-0.03 of population mass per period) and are staircase in policy size at any finite Nb, compounding the standing Nb-convergence finding — and (ii) the kappa_fert narrative — the smoothing parameter governs only the interior 60% of childless-fertile mass, with extensive-margin moments loading on c_bar_n/h_bar_jump through feasibility. If the lead's taxonomy allows, classify as MAJOR (interpretation/policy caveat, not defect); if the paper nowhere claims interior probabilities everywhere and no smooth fertility elasticity is quoted, MINOR-plus is defensible for the estimation itself. The separate uniform-1/3 fert_probs on fully-invalid populated nodes (mass 0.0172) is a distinct artifact and should not be folded into this finding.

verdict_entries.reasoning:
The factual core of T2 is correct, but its causal/economic narrative is partially wrong and the H0-specific piece is second-order; the finding earns MAJOR only through the aggregate at-bound pattern, not through H0.

Facts verified: (1) current_bound_best has H0 = 9.999672059746388 in [1,10] — within 0.0033% of the cap (the CSV's at_bound=False for H0 is a labeling artifact of not landing exactly on 10; near_bound_2pct=True). The pattern search clips trial points to the unit cube (local_panel.py:727), so the search literally cannot explore H0>10; an estimate hugging 10 means the constraint is active. (2) Four parameters are flagged at_bound=True (c_bar_0=1.28 upper, chi=1.15 upper, h_bar_0=1.0 lower, tenure_choice_kappa=0 lower), so 5 of 14 are effectively at/near bounds. (3) The rooms moment undershoots: 5.5511 vs 5.7800, contribution 0.3143 = 2.13% of the 14.780 loss.

Where the narrative fails: the claim "the model wants more housing supply than the bound permits, so the aggregate-rooms target is unreached for a constraint reason, not an economic one" is contradicted by the overnight's own housing_relaxed arm. Both wave-2 arms used the IDENTICAL H0 bound [1,10] (run_wave2_lateral.py:51 and :142; run_overnight_combined.py:73-77 shows housing_relaxed only widens h_bar_0/h_bar_jump/h_bar_n/chi). In that arm the optimizer moved H0 INTERIOR to 8.5597 (1.44 units of unused headroom, near_bound=False), achieved a better loss (13.9035 vs 14.7800, a 5.9% improvement), and the rooms moment STILL undershot by almost the same amount (5.5851, gap -0.1949 vs -0.2289). So the rooms undershoot survives with slack in H0: it is a weight-6 moment losing a trade-off against the weight-100/160 ownership moments that already overshoot (own_rate +0.078, old_age_own_rate +0.184) and would worsen with cheaper housing — an economic trade-off, not a bound artifact. H0=10 in the strict arm is best read as substitution for the binding chi=1.15 and h_bar_0=1.0 corners, not as data demanding more supply.

Magnitude check: even fully closing the rooms gap is worth at most 0.31 of 14.78 (2.1%); extending the H0 bound to 20 would plausibly buy less than the 0.88 already available from relaxing the chi/h_bar bounds. So the H0-specific severity is MINOR-scale. What IS material: (a) 5/14 parameters at corners means the reported "just-identified 15/14" spec is really a bound-constrained optimum whose binding search-region edges act as unstated external restrictions — and the relaxed arm proves they bind with economically meaningful multipliers (loss -0.88; own_rate_2534 gap improves from -0.0906 to +0.0057 when chi/h_bar relax); (b) any policy counterfactual run at the strict candidate inherits corner values of chi, c_bar_0, h_bar_0, tenure_choice_kappa and a near-corner H0. Note c_bar_0 was already 1.2793 (at the cap) in FROZEN_SOURCE_THETA (production_profile.py:56-58), so several corners predate this overnight and are properties of the production spec, not last-minute damage. Reporting infrastructure exists (near_bound_2pct/at_bound columns), so the CLAUDE.md at-bound reporting requirement is partially satisfied already, though the H0 row's at_bound=False is misleading.

Decisive test status: the proposed bound-extension rerun is the right test. I launched the cheap local version (fixed strict theta, H0 in {8.56, 10, 12, 16}, Nb=40) via output/model/full_audit_20260711/scripts/verify_T2_h0_bound.py; it was still solving its first equilibrium case single-threaded when this verdict was due, and will write output/model/full_audit_20260711/verify_T2/h0_sweep_nb40.json (process PID 83459). The verdict does not depend on it: the Nb=120 cross-arm evidence above is stronger than any Nb=40 derivative. Cluster-side case-level JSONLs (the 23,830 strict records) are on scratch and blocked by the SSH outage.
verdict_entries.severity_adjustment: Keep MAJOR, but re-scope the finding. As written ("supply scale not pinned; rooms target unreached for a constraint reason") it is overstated: the housing_relaxed arm — same H0 cap — puts H0 interior at 8.56 with a better loss and a nearly identical rooms undershoot, so the rooms miss is an economic trade-off against the heavily weighted, already-overshooting ownership moments (rooms is only 2.1% of the loss). The H0 piece alone is MINOR. The MAJOR content is the aggregate corner pattern: 5 of 14 parameters at/near bounds, with the relaxed arm proving the chi/h_bar bounds bind at 0.88 loss units (5.9%) and flip the own_rate_2534 fit — those bounds function as unreported external restrictions on the estimated model, and policy runs at the strict candidate inherit the corners. Recommended reporting fix: state the candidate as bound-constrained, flag H0's at_bound=False label as misleading (it is 3.3e-5 from the cap on a 9-unit range), and justify or relax the chi/c_bar_0/h_bar_0 edges before any policy use.

verdict_entries.reasoning:
The finding is CONFIRMED at FATAL severity, with one quantitative caveat about the counterfactual and one minor overstatement about the gradient.

1. Code mechanism verified. solver.py:2305-2311 builds the fertility choice over Vfa and applies a plain softmax with no feasibility mask; utils.py:194-199 logsumexp has no sentinel handling. The KFE at solver.py:3762-3777 multiplies ALL childless mass by these probabilities ("gc = g[:, :, :, j, zz, 0, 0] ... mbp = gc[:, :, :, None] * pa") with no invalid-node exclusion, and counts the resulting births in total_births.

2. Independent smoking gun (my own check, not the diagnostics auditor's): at the flagged nodes V_childless = -9999999997.922932 = -1e10 + 2.0770698, and the candidate theta has kappa_fert = 1.8906304, giving kappa_fert*ln(3) = 2.0770698 — an exact match to the fert-stage entropy bonus kappa*log(#options). This proves all three fertility options at those nodes are EXACTLY the -1e10 sentinel, so the probabilities are uniform 1/3 by construction (0.333333485... each; the deviation from exact 1/3 is float rounding at 1e10 magnitude). This is a pure numerical default, not a smoothed choice.

3. Magnitudes verified from the diagnostics artifacts plus my own arithmetic:
- Entering cohort mass = sum of j0_mass_by_bnode = 0.058824 = 1/17. Invalid-childless entrant mass at j=0 = 0.011986 → 20.4% of the cohort, exactly as claimed.
- Uniform split: invalid j=0 mass by parity is exactly [0.003995329733568752]*3. Artifact children = 0.003995*1 + 0.003995*2 = 0.011986.
- Total births per cohort = mean_completed_fertility (0.9915795, summary.json) x 0.0588235 = 0.058328. Artifact share = 20.55% of all births. The "~21%" claim is correct.
- TFR = 2*mean_parity (solver.py:1705) with mean_parity from parity_dist over post-fertile ages (solver.py:4661-4665); no attrition, so artifact households carry n=1,2 to measurement ages. Mechanical removal gives TFR 1.9832 - 2*(0.011986/0.0588235) = 1.5756. The "~1.57" claim is arithmetically exact as an upper-bound counterfactual.
- Both reported candidates are affected: populated_invalid_share is 0.01719 (current_bound, 14.780) and 0.01742 (relaxed_light, 13.903), same order.

4. Loss materiality (active target set candidate_replacement_post_audit_v1, resolved live): tfr target 1.918 weight 20; childless_rate target 0.188 weight 20. Current tfr loss contribution is 20*(1.9832-1.918)^2 = 0.085. Under mechanical removal it becomes 20*(1.5756-1.918)^2 = 2.34, i.e. +2.26 on a total loss of 14.78 (+15%) from ONE moment. childless_rate (model 0.1713, already below target) would rise toward an upper bound of 0.1713 + (2/3)*0.2038 = 0.307, worth up to +2.8 more loss. The reported fit of the fertility block is artifact-supported, and the direction flatters the fit exactly as claimed.

5. Policy consequence stands and is arguably the strongest FATAL leg: uniform probabilities on all-sentinel nodes are invariant to any price, subsidy, or housing-cost change (as long as the nodes stay infeasible), so ~1/5 of baseline births cannot respond to any policy. For a paper whose object is the housing-cost elasticity of fertility, baseline fertility levels and policy elasticities are both biased (elasticities toward zero) by construction.

6. Caveats that do NOT rescue the candidate: (a) the -0.41 TFR delta is an upper bound — invalid mass collapses from 0.011986 at j=0 to 0.00316 at j=1 (probe_checks populated_invalid.by_age), so in a corrected model these households become feasible within one period and would recover some births at j>=1; the net TFR effect of a principled fix is smaller than 0.41 but still an order of magnitude larger than the current 0.065 fit gap, and it moves childless_rate, age composition, and parent wealth states regardless. (b) The problem is jointly an entrant-placement issue: 20% of entrants start at b as low as -2.91 (inherited-debt nodes) where even childless subsistence is infeasible; the fert logit on sentinels is the symptom. This broadens rather than reduces severity. (c) Minor overstatement: the fertility-income gradient contamination is weak — per-capita invalid entrant shares by z are roughly uniform (~0.26, 0.20, 0.20, 0.20, 0.20), so the artifact adds ~0.2 children roughly uniformly across z, compressing the relative gradient slightly; and the gradient is not in the target set, so no loss effect. This does not change the verdict.

Bottom line: ~21% of births in both reported candidates are a numerical default that directly supports the two fertility targets (combined weight 40) and damps the paper's central policy elasticity. The estimated fertility parameters (psi_child 0.2691, kappa_fert 1.8906, c_bar_n 0.456, h_bar_n 0.9841) are conditional on the artifact; neither the 14.780 nor the 13.903 candidate is an optimum of the intended model. FATAL is the correct classification for the estimated model and any fertility-side result; re-estimation after a fix (mask infeasible options in the fert logit AND resolve why entrants populate all-infeasible debt nodes) is required. The proposed decisive test (zero out fert rows where V<=-1e9 and re-extract moments) is well-posed as an upper-bound demonstration; the full fix additionally needs the entrant-placement question answered.

verdict_entries.severity_adjustment: Keep FATAL. The one inflation in the original write-up — treating the full -0.41 TFR delta as the corrected-model prediction rather than an upper bound (invalid mass decays to 0.0032 by j=1, so some births would be recovered at later ages in a proper fix) and the weak gradient-contamination claim — reduces the point estimate of the damage but not the classification: even a 50% recovery leaves the tfr moment mismeasured by ~0.2 (3x the target's distance to model), leaves ~1/5 of baseline births policy-inelastic, and invalidates the estimated fertility parameter block in both candidates. Reproducibility is unaffected (the artifact is deterministic); it is the economic validity of the estimates and policy elasticities that fails.

verdict_entries.reasoning:
The code characterization is correct and I reproduced the mechanism independently.

(1) Fert softmax has no feasibility mask. solver.py:2304-2312 (markov-income Bellman, the audited path): Vfa is assembled from VI (childless at cs=0, n>=1 at cs=1) and then `lf = Vfa / P.kappa_fert; ls, pr = logsumexp(lf, axis=3); fert_probs[:, :, :, j, zz, :] = pr`. logsumexp (utils.py:194-199) is a plain softmax: when all npar=3 options carry the -1e10 infeasibility sentinel (set at solver.py:2266 `Vrs[inf_m, nn, cs] = -1e10` and propagated through tenure/location logits), pr is ~(1/3,1/3,1/3). The recorded value 0.333333485667725 (not exact 1/3, rows summing to 1.0000005) is float cancellation at the -1e10 scale in `probs = np.exp(a - ls)`, confirming the inputs are all at sentinel magnitude. There is no deterministic/argmax branch for fertility and no validity screen anywhere in the block.

(2) The KFE applies these probabilities to populated infeasible nodes. Entrants are injected at renter/n=0/cs=0 over grid nodes from an empirical wealth/income-ratio distribution with no feasibility check (solver.py:3710-3715, `g[int(kk), 0, i, 0, zz, 0, 0] += ...`), so low-z negative-wealth entrants land on nodes where even remaining childless is infeasible (V = -1e10 + kappa_fert*ln3 = -9999999997.92, matching the JSON). The reshuffle (solver.py:3762-3777) then computes `ba_j = gc * np.sum(pa * pv, axis=3); total_births += ...; mbp = gc[...,None] * pa` and moves a third of that mass each to n=1 and n=2 — with A_f_start=1 (parameters.py:33) the condition `j+1 >= P.A_f_start` includes j=0. The locate_invalid_j0.json rows show exactly this signature: identical masses at (n=0,cs=0), (n=1,cs=1), (n=2,cs=1) on each invalid b-node.

(3) The moments inherit the artifact. parity_dist/mean_parity integrate g over ages >= A_f_end with no validity mask (solver.py:4577-4580), and the calibration moment is `"tfr": 2.0 * household_parity` with household_parity = mean_completed_fertility = mean_parity (calibration.py:1082,1093; solver.py:1484). childless_rate = parity_dist[0] likewise.

(4) Magnitudes. The diagnostics packet's Nb=120 numbers are internally consistent and I re-verified the arithmetic: invalid populated j=0 mass 0.011986 (extra_checks.json, exactly [0.003995...]*3 by n — the uniform-split signature), entrant cohort mass 1/17=0.058824 (20.4% of entrants), gross artifact births 0.011986 vs total births = mean_parity*cohort mass = 0.9916*0.058824 = 0.05833, i.e. 20.5% ~ "21% of all births" as claimed (gross).

(5) Decisive independent test (mine, Nb=40 PE at the candidate theta and price): reran the production forward_distribution_markov_income twice from my own script — baseline exactly reproduces the solver stats; forcing fert_probs=[1,0,0] at nodes with V[...,0,0] <= -1e9 gives TFR 1.8513 -> 1.6517 (-0.20), childless_rate 0.2520 -> 0.2986 (+0.047), total births -10.8%. Output: output/model/full_audit_20260711/verify_F1/verify_diag1_fert_artifact_nb40.json.

One quantitative caveat (does not overturn the finding): the finding's "TFR net of the artifact ~1.57" subtracts the gross j=0 artifact births. A proper counterfactual (my Nb=40 test) shows the net effect is roughly half the gross — masked households remain childless, age into feasible states, and then have genuine births (at Nb=40: gross artifact 22% of births, net 10.8%; net TFR bias -0.20, not -0.40). So "~21% of births are mechanical at j=0" is correct as a gross accounting statement, but the implied corrected TFR is overstated; the Nb=120 net correction is plausibly ~0.2, still first-order against target tfr=1.918 with weight 20, and the childless_rate contamination (~+0.05 when corrected, i.e. model 0.171 is understated) is also first-order. Note also the fitted equilibrium price would move once ~20% of entrant mass stops mechanically acquiring children, so the fitted moment vector and the fertility-block parameter estimates are contaminated either way. Cluster-side confirmation at Nb=120/240 is blocked (SSH unavailable) but unnecessary for the code-correctness verdict.
verdict_entries.checked_lines: solver.py:2266 "Vrs[inf_m, nn, cs] = -1e10" (infeasibility sentinel); solver.py:2304-2312 "lf = Vfa / P.kappa_fert" / "ls, pr = logsumexp(lf, axis=3)" / "fert_probs[:, :, :, j, zz, :] = pr" (no feasibility mask); solver.py:2313 "V[:, :, :, j, zz, 0, 0] = fert_value[:, :, :, j, zz]" (explains V=-9999999997.92 = -1e10 + kappa_fert*ln 3); utils.py:194-199 "m = np.max(a, axis=axis...); probs = np.exp(a - ls)" (plain softmax, uniform on equal inputs); solver.py:3710-3715 "g[int(kk), 0, i, 0, zz, 0, 0] += float(ww) * P.entry_by_loc[i] * z_weights[zz]" (entrant injection, no feasibility screen); solver.py:3762-3777 "pa = fert_probs[:, :, :, j, zz, :]" / "ba_j = gc * np.sum(pa * pv, axis=3)" / "total_births += float(np.sum(ba_j))" / "g[:, :, :, j, zz, 0, 0] = 0.0" (unmasked reshuffle incl. j=0 since parameters.py:33 "P.A_f_start = 1"); solver.py:4577-4580 "stats.parity_dist[nn] = np.sum(g[:, :, :, P.A_f_end :, :, nn, :])..." / "stats.mean_parity = float(np.sum(np.arange(npar) * stats.parity_dist))" (no validity mask); solver.py:1484-1485 mean_completed_fertility/childless_rate wiring; calibration.py:1093 "\"tfr\": 2.0 * household_parity"; solver.py:1261 forward_distribution_markov_income call site (audited markov path); diagnostics evidence read: output/model/full_audit_20260711/diagnostics/current_bound/locate_invalid_j0.json (uniform fert_probs rows, equal-thirds masses) and extra_checks.json ("invalid_populated_j0": {"by_parity": [0.003995329733568752]*3, "total": 0.011985989200706258}); my independent counterfactual: output/model/full_audit_20260711/scripts/verify_diag1_fert_artifact.py and verify_F1/verify_diag1_fert_artifact_nb40.json (TFR 1.8513->1.6517, childless 0.2520->0.2986, births -10.8% when infeasible-node fertility is masked; baseline rerun reproduces solver stats exactly).
verdict_entries.severity_adjustment: Keep FATAL for the fertility block, with one correction to the stated magnitude: the ~21% figure is the GROSS share of births generated on infeasible nodes at j=0 and is verified; but the finding's implied corrected TFR (~1.57, i.e. -0.41) double-counts, because in a corrected model those households remain childless and later have genuine births. My Nb=40 counterfactual puts the NET bias at roughly half the gross (TFR -0.20, childless_rate +0.047, births -10.8%). Even at the net magnitude the contamination is far outside target tolerance (tfr target 1.918 weight 20; childless_rate targeted), it is systematic rather than noise, and the fertility parameters (psi_child, kappa_fert, c_bar_n, h_bar_n) were estimated against contaminated moments — so the classification FATAL-for-fertility-estimates stands, but the lead should quote the net (~0.2 TFR, ~0.05 childlessness) rather than gross (~0.4) correction, pending an Nb=120 masked-KFE rerun for the exact number.

verdict_entries.reasoning:
CONFIRMED at MAJOR; the severity is not inflated — if anything the diagnostics auditor understated the loudest channel. The mechanics check out exactly as claimed: solver.py:3715 injects entry mass onto whatever grid nodes the PSID income-ratio distribution implies ("g[int(kk), 0, i, 0, zz, 0, 0] += float(ww) * P.entry_by_loc[i] * z_weights[zz]"), with no feasibility screen, and at both candidates c_bar_0 sits exactly at the search upper bound 1.28 (production_profile.py:42, "(\"c_bar_0\", 0.020 * PERIOD_YEARS, 0.320 * PERIOD_YEARS)"; current theta c_bar_0=1.28, housing theta c_bar_0=1.28), so ~20.4% of each entering cohort lands on V=-1e10 renter nodes (probe_checks.json populated_invalid by_age[0]=0.011986 vs cohort mass ~1/17=0.0588).

Materiality decomposes into one first-order channel and several second-order ones:

FIRST-ORDER — fertility. The fertility default at infeasible nodes is not vaguely "undefined": the softmax at solver.py:2309-2311 ("lf = Vfa / P.kappa_fert; ls, pr = logsumexp(lf, axis=3)") degenerates to uniform [1/3,1/3,1/3] over {0,1,2} births when all parity options carry the -1e10 sentinel, and the KFE at solver.py:3766-3775 applies these probabilities to the injected mass ("pa = fert_probs[:, :, :, j, zz, :] ... mbp = gc[:, :, :, None] * pa"). diagnostics/current_bound/locate_invalid_j0.json shows exactly this in the stationary distribution: each invalid j=0 (b,z) node carries identical mass at n=0, n=1, n=2 with fert_probs=[0.3333,0.3333,0.3333]. So roughly 14% of every cohort is assigned children by a coin flip at ages 18-21. The decisive counterfactual has already been run (verify_F1/verify_diag1_fert_artifact_nb40.json, PE at the candidate's p_eq, Nb=40, forcing stay-childless on sentinel nodes): TFR 1.851 -> 1.652 (-0.20), childless_rate 0.252 -> 0.299 (+4.7pp), artifact_birth_share_of_total = 10.8%. Against the candidate's target gaps (tfr gap +0.065 at weight 20; childless_rate gap -0.017 at weight 20 in repro/current_repro_exact.json audit_fit), the artifact moves TFR by ~3x the reported gap and childlessness by ~3x the reported gap. The fertility block's apparent fit is therefore materially artifact-supported: ~11% of all births in the estimated model are uniform-random draws on infeasibility sentinels, and the untargeted mean_age_first_birth (30.56) is being pulled down by births that occur at ages 18-21 for non-economic reasons. Knock-ons touch every composition-dependent target: own_family_gap (w=45), parity shares, housing_increment_0to1 (birth-event cohorts include coin-flip births).

SECOND-ORDER — housing/wealth moments. Direct contamination of the other targets is small: invalid mass in the 25-34 window (j=2..4) is 0.00167, ~0.95% of the window, and 99.5% of invalid mass is renter (by_tenure[0]=0.017115 of 0.017195), so own_rate_2534 bias is bounded by ~0.9pp against a -9.1pp gap (loss shift ~0.1 of 14.78). aggregate_mean_occupied_rooms_18_85 (w=6) carries 1.72% default-policy room choices, bias bounded ~±0.05 rooms vs gap -0.229 (loss shift ~0.15). young_childless_renter LW/income: invalid childless mass in the window is <=0.00167 and mostly at j=0-1 outside the 25-35 window; effect order 0.05 on a +0.364 gap. None of these alone changes interpretation.

ENTRY-VALUE POISONING — inert for this objective. entry_values_by_location/city_entry_probabilities (solver.py:344, 443) are only used by the entry-margin closure functions, and local_panel.py contains no reference to them (rg over local_panel.py returns nothing); I=1 (parameters.py:109 "P.I = 1") makes location entry choice degenerate anyway. This channel matters only for later entry-margin/policy work (already a known gotcha), not for the 14.780/13.903 candidates.

COMPARABILITY AND SEARCH DISTORTION. Both candidates were evaluated under identical code and both sit at c_bar_0=1.28, so the 0.877 loss gap between them is unlikely to be an artifact of differential invalid mass (their thetas are close). But the artifact plausibly explains WHY c_bar_0 rides its upper bound: raising c_bar_0 enlarges the infeasible entrant set and manufactures free births that improve tfr and childless_rate simultaneously — a non-economic gradient the DE search could exploit. That specific claim is SUSPECTED (mechanism demonstrated, gradient direction not separately measured), but it means c_bar_0, kappa_fert, psi_child and c_bar_n estimates should not be trusted as economic quantities until re-estimated with the artifact closed (mass shifted to nearest feasible node, or stay-childless default on sentinel nodes).

Severity: MAJOR stands. Not FATAL for the whole model — housing/tenure/wealth moments are directly contaminated only at the ~1% level and both candidates remain internally comparable — but this is at the MAJOR/FATAL boundary for the fertility block specifically: any paper statement about fertility levels, childlessness, timing, or the fertility-parameter estimates at these candidates reflects, in material part (~11% of births), uniform-random assignment on infeasible states. Escalation test: re-run the local polish with the sentinel-node fertility masked (or entry mass reallocated to nearest feasible node) and check whether theta and the fitted fertility moments move materially; if yes, FATAL for the reported candidates' fertility interpretation.
