verdict_entries.severity_adjustment: Keep MAJOR, but sharpen its content: the generic "undefined default policies" framing undersells the binding channel and oversells the rest. (1) Fertility is the first-order casualty and is borderline FATAL for the fertility block: 10.8% of all births in the estimated model are uniform-random 1/3-1/3-1/3 draws on sentinel nodes (measured counterfactual: TFR -0.20, childlessness +4.7pp — each ~3x the corresponding target gap at weight 20), and both reported candidates sit exactly at the c_bar_0 upper search bound that maximizes this free-births channel, so c_bar_0/kappa_fert/psi_child/c_bar_n estimates are suspect. (2) The non-fertility contamination emphasized in the finding (own_rate_2534, young liquid wealth, rooms) is second-order: bounded at ~0.9pp on own_rate_2534 (vs a 9.1pp gap), ~0.05 rooms on the 18-85 rooms target (vs a 0.23 gap), total loss arithmetic ~0.1-0.6 out of 14.78. (3) Entry-value poisoning is inert for these candidates (I=1, no entry-margin closure in the local_panel objective path) and should not be counted toward severity here. Escalate to FATAL (fertility interpretation of the reported candidates) if a re-polish with the sentinel-fertility mask moves theta or the fertility moments materially.

summaries.v2:a0b09e2bea2862ac12e15df0a93988e012d44: Provenance manifest built. HEAD is fe092ca (2026-07-10, main); the working tree is dirty and the dirty tree, not HEAD, is the audited object. Five working-tree modifications to tracked files in code/model/intergen_housing_fertility/ are load-bearing for the 14.780 candidate (local_panel.py, solver.py, calibration.py, parameters.py, cli.py), and every driver that produced the overnight — the repo driver run_intergen_combined_recalibration.py, both combined submit scripts, and all seven files in tmp/overnight_combined_20260710/ — is untracked; the result artifacts under output/model/combined_recalibration/ are git-ignored. Verdict: the candidate is NOT reproducible from committed source alone. At HEAD the drivers would crash (run_local_polish lacks the additional_search_bounds/additional_targets/fixed_model_overrides/normalize_bequest_utility kwargs), and even absent that, HEAD merges **income BEFORE extra_overrides in run_local_panel_case, so the production profile's stale 5-point z-grid would silently override the Rouwenhorst income — a different economy. The repo driver and the tmp overnight drivers are materially different programs: the repo driver hard-caps evals at 160 and minutes at 60, while wave 1 ran 850 evals/330 min with per-task jitter and per-arm bounds relaxation applied by mutating the module global lp.GLOBAL_DE_BOUNDS; wave-1 metadata marks relaxed arms diagnostic_only but wave 2 reuses the same relaxed bounds without that marker. One new collateral observation: write_best_case_diagnostics (panel path only, not the polish path) solves without the profile/fixed-spec overrides, so panel-generated diagnostics can be computed under a different spec than the ranked record. Cluster-side snapshot diff is blocked (no SSH). All hashes (37 files) are in hashes.json; full detail in PROVENANCE_MANIFEST.md.
summaries.v2:05c2888b0048ed8bcfd650b18b9bdee9b298c: Every quantitative object in latex/intergenerational_housing_fertility_paper_draft.tex is a single, internally consistent vintage: the 16.13 "circulation candidate" (output/model/intergen_circulation_candidate_20260711, record pattern_i003_d08_m, loss 16.13191908615311, P=0.7816130048883417, solved under the combined corrected spec). The parameter table, all 15 fit-table moments, the 2.39e-5 residual, the mechanism statistics (0.178/0.130/0.307/2.37), the family-stock model column (2.4/16.9/43.5/37.2/80.7/74.8), mean age at first birth 30.9, and both repaired figures (byte-identical via cmp to the circulation-candidate copies) all match that directory digit-for-digit. No 6.31-era numbers and no vintage mixing were found in the paper. Because the 16.13 candidate uses the same 15 targets and identical weights as the 14.78 current_bound_best, the two ARE loss-comparable and everything is stale by 1.35 loss units; several fit narratives flip sign at 14.78 (own_rate goes from undershooting 0.509 to overshooting 0.653; childlessness flips from over to under), and the at-bound parameter set changes (psi_child and kappa_fert become interior, h_bar_0 hits its lower bound, H0 sits at 9.9997 against upper bound 10), so the fit prose needs rewriting, not renumbering. The policy numbers (+3.4%/+4.9% births, +0.66%/-18.80% price, in abstract, intro, Section 7, and conclusion) come from the phase9b entry-margin battery at the same 16.13 theta; the source README flags "Baseline validation: FAIL" and "fixed-theta diagnostics, not recalibrated SMM losses", and the paper silently reports the fixed-scale closure column while the same table's scaled/published columns give price effects of +1.56/-17.80 and +1.34/-11.16 — closure-variant sensitivity up to factor 2.4 on the grant's price effect is undisclosed. On the generator scripts: only build_intergen_mechanics_packet.py can apply the combined spec, and only via an opt-in flag that ignores the flag stored in the candidate record itself (silent stale-spec rebuild is one omitted flag away, with hR_max falling back to 8.0); regen_lifecycle_compare_notitle.py and run_policy_counterfactuals_from_record.py re-solve through the dt_cp two-location strand and cannot apply the spec at all; the two cache plotters default to a June-27 pre-spec cache and a nonexistent path respectively. The untracked issues ledger is stale in two entries (BQ-2 and HF-4 describe the pre-combined-spec code; the paper and both candidates now normalize bequest utility and estimate H0 with eta=1.75). Full inventory written to output/model/full_audit_20260711/paper_audit/RESULTS_STALENESS.md.
summaries.v2:7cccaeca1848e29454f1e42d3c1903af380af: Bequest audit: the normalized warm-glow bequest used by the July 10 overnight is implemented correctly. solver.py:5604-5620 (bequest_utility_vec) matches the paper display B(W,n)=theta0*(1+theta_n*n)*[(theta1+max{W,0})^(1-sigma)-theta1^(1-sigma)]/(1-sigma) term by term (numeric check exact to 1e-13); B(0,n)=0 under the flag; monotone in W including the negative-estate clip; sigma is fixed at 2.0 and never searched, so the sigma=1 log branch is an unreachable but correct guard. Gross estate is b + p*H at full market value (gross of mortgage debt, no sale haircut, estate tax machinery present but dormant at rate 0, applied before utility if ever nonzero) — exactly the paper's "no forced sale, no transaction cost, no tax at death". Family-size scaling uses the encoded index n in {0,1,2}, which the paper explicitly declares is the intended unit ("bequests ... evaluated in index units"), so no doubling mismatch. Both Bellman variants (markov-income and core) feed Vbq as the J-1 continuation; there is no pre-terminal death; apply_child_aging at the terminal boundary leaves completed fertility invariant so the bequest scale is unaffected. The overnight chain sets normalize_bequest_utility=True redundantly (kwarg + fixed_model_overrides) in both waves and the merge order preserves it; the unnormalized branch is reachable only when the flag is False, as intended. All 7 tests in test_bequest_normalization.py pass (via unittest; pytest is not installed in the venv). The one material finding is a MAJOR staleness trap: the flag defaults to False (parameters.py:66) and eight intergen re-solve tools plus every CLI subcommand except local-polish never set it (or the rest of the combined fixed spec), so any post-hoc replay of the combined-spec best through those entry points silently reverts to the old unnormalized bequest — a family-size-dependent terminal-utility drop of 13.2/23.3/33.4 utils at n=0/1/2 at the live candidate theta (theta0=0.1318, theta_n=0.7679, theta1=0.01). Secondary notes: per-record best.json does not store the flag/overrides (only run-level meta does), and the July 9 citation solver.py:5067-5076 for the old form is stale (function now at 5604-5620). One economics flag for the identification adjudicator: with theta1=0.01 the bequest motive saturates at tiny estates (98% of its upper bound by W=0.5), making theta_n identified mostly by a level jump rather than saving slopes. Worksheet: output/model/full_audit_20260711/spec_audit/BEQUEST_AUDIT.md.
summaries.v2:d6b8d721ec5a382f7b2bc4a79357c777b816f: Search-integrity audit of the July-10 overnight combined-spec calibration (waves 13313018/13314033, reducer 13314034), local evidence only. The bounds-patching mechanics are sound: bounds_for_arm('baseline_pattern') returns exactly PRODUCTION_SEARCH_BOUNDS (verified by value comparison), the relaxed arms only substitute the four named preference/housing boxes, and the report parameter CSVs list the actual per-arm search bounds. Strict gating is airtight at every selection point (in-run best, select_seed, reducer); non-strict records are ignored, and the failure mode when zero strict records exist is a crash, not silent masking. The override merge in run_local_panel_case puts income after the profile overrides in both wave-2 code paths, independently confirming the lead's finding. The headline finding is about effective search breadth and provenance: the two reported candidates (current_bound_best 14.78, housing_relaxed_best 13.90) share beta, c_bar_n, theta0 to 16 digits across different arms and tasks, and their remaining coordinate differences decompose exactly into wave-2 pattern-polish steps (H0 diff = 2x0.08x9 = 1.44; alpha_cons = 1x0.08x0.55; kappa_fert = 1x0.08x0.6^4x11) — proving both descend from the single shared wave-1 seed via units[0], and that the 14-D, ~1,416-draw wave-2 LHS "global" stage never beat that lineage. Furthermore, current_bound_best sits at exactly the three production-box boundaries (c_bar_0=1.28, chi=1.15, h_bar_0=1.0) where housing_relaxed_best is interior to the relaxed box with h_bar_0<1 — representable only in the housing-relaxed box — so the shared wave-1 seed almost surely came from the wave-1 housing_relaxed arm, which wave-1's own launch metadata labels diagnostic_only, and was silently clipped onto the production box by global_unit_from_theta's np.clip (verified to project out-of-box thetas without error). The candidates are still valid strictly-converged local optima with correct losses, but any "global search over the stated bounds" claim is unsupported, and current_bound_best's at-bound parameters are clipping artifacts, not search verdicts. Metadata under patching is mildly self-contradictory: meta['source_controlled_bounds'] equals the patched box (mislabeled) while production_profile_spec.bounds shows the unpatched one; the actual box is correctly in meta['bounds']. Wave-2 budget arithmetic (92.4-min global deadline vs 60 draws) fits even at 2x the 37 s solve time, so truncation is unlikely but only checkable via scratch lateral_summary.json. Blocked by SSH: OVERNIGHT_SEED_ROOT values, per-task source_seed_path, global_completed counts, cases.jsonl, cross_arm_summary.json, and smoke-run execution evidence.
summaries.v2:95d5aa7e45e92337acd5b7b77e264a1d6e5b5: Model-side target/objective audit of the 15-moment system (candidate_replacement_post_audit_v1 + aggregate_mean_occupied_rooms_18_85 @ w=6) is complete. The objective arithmetic is clean: diagnostic_loss is exactly sum_i w_i*(m_i-t_i)^2 in raw units with a +100 penalty only above residual 5e-3 (inactive here), and both stored losses (14.780020699972585 and 13.90346531862862) reproduce to the exact float from the stored fit arrays and from calibration.py's target/weight constants; every stored gap and contribution is exact. All 15 model counterparts were traced into solver.py with line-level verification of age windows (18+4j grid; 25-34 -> model ages 26-34 period starts; 65-75 -> 66-74, all retired; 30-55 -> 30-54), conditioning sets (two different "childless" definitions coexist: childless-in-household incl. empty-nesters for room/young-wealth moments vs never-parent for the old wealth gap), and income denominators (young moment: annual gross labor income z-resolved; old moments: annual pension, correctly annual despite a stale ledger note claiming period income). The main problems are not arithmetic but design: old_age_own_rate (w=160) alone is 36-46% of both candidates' losses so the weight vector, which is hand-set and never variance-justified, effectively decides the estimate; the hard-targeted old nonhousing wealth MEDIAN is an uninterpolated b-grid value (piecewise-constant in parameters), violating the standing never-hard-target-medians rule; H0, the one newly searched parameter, ends at 9.9997 of its [1,10] bound while its identifying moment still undershoots; and theta_n remains disciplined by a single 2%-of-loss moment, with the two near-equal-loss candidates differing 0.77 vs 1.11 in theta_n — D11's weak identification is not resolved by theta0 now being interior. With c_bar_0, h_bar_0, chi at bounds and H0 effectively at bound, the effective interior parameter count is ~9-10 against 15 moments, so the nominal 15>=14 identification count overstates what the data disciplines.
summaries.v2:107ca8423aa3f26fb62504882b6a3418ef938: The rewritten draft (dirty tree) DOES describe the corrected combined-spec code: 5-state Rouwenhorst with annual rho 0.960/sd 0.0645 and rho_4y=0.85 (paper grid {0.613,...,1.546} reproduces the code construction exactly), the normalized bequest form theta0(1+theta_n n)[((theta1+W)^(1-sigma)-theta1^(1-sigma))/(1-sigma)] on the gross estate b+Ph with no estate tax, the full finance block (2%/1.1%/4x1%/phi=0.80 with (1-phi) down payment and -phi PH debt floor), the eta=1.75 supply curve with estimated H0 targeting ACS 5.780, the residual owner-service form chi(H-hbar) (July 9 decision, matches kernels.py), the {2,4,6,8,10}/cap-6/J=17/entry-18 configuration, the post-decision measurement, the 2E[n] units-crosswalk footnote, and exactly the 15 moments/14 parameters of the production target system. However, all quantitative tables and figures transcribe the FROZEN circulation candidate (output/model/intergen_circulation_candidate_20260711, pattern_i003_d08_m, loss 16.13192, residual 2.3904e-5, Nb=120) — verified exact on all 14 parameters, all 15 moments, mechanism numbers, family-stock table, mean age at first birth 30.907, and byte-identical figure SHA1s — not the overnight strict best current_bound_best (loss 14.78002) with materially different parameters (H0 at the 10 upper bound vs 7.51 interior, psi_child 0.269 vs 0.35, theta0 0.132 vs 0.221) and a materially different fit. Second substantive problem: 4 of 14 reported estimates sit exactly on undisclosed search bounds (c_bar_0, psi_child, chi upper; kappa_fert lower) with h_bar_0 within 2% of its lower bound, but the paper discloses only kappa_T's bound plus a vague footnote, while the identification prose treats the bound-pinned values as interior estimates. Third: the paper's numbers are Nb=120 solves never re-verified at the production Nb=240 grid and Nb is undisclosed. Remaining gaps are reproducibility omissions (income age-profile values, r_bar=0.16, search bounds, entry-block calibration, fit-table weights). No FATAL paper-vs-code contradiction found. Full table and required-changes list written to output/model/full_audit_20260711/paper_audit/PAPER_SPEC_AUDIT.md.
summaries.v2:fd84ea524e7efcb52f1df5ed3601c1ad80472: The July 9 timing repair is CORRECT on the audited dirty tree, verified from first principles and by a decisive runnable identity test (all checks pass). (1) The Bellman gives period-j flow utility from the period-j chosen tenure/housing and same-period child state; the KFE nesting order (fertility -> location -> tenure -> savings -> aging) exactly matches. (2) With use_postdecision_current_distribution=True (default True in parameters.py:100-101, so no tool silently runs old timing), the stationary distribution used for BOTH moments and market clearing is the single post-choice object g_current = realize_current_cross_section(g); my test shows recomputed demand from the returned distribution equals sol.housing_demand to 1.8e-15, mass is conserved age-by-age and by (age,n,cs) to 8.7e-17 (the in-solver assert is aggregate-only), and reported own-by-age equals a manual application of the age-j tenure policy to the age-j beginning mass to 1.5e-15 — while the legacy flag-False series is exactly the one-period lag, i.e., the pre-repair defect existed and is gone. (3) Births increment n, child costs, and the child housing floor at j itself, and birth-triggered same-period purchases are counted in both the stationary moments and the horizon-0 event-study DiD (advance_cohort with horizon 0 is the identity). Four residual issues, none invalidating the overnight results: the July 2 age-window finding is STILL PRESENT (own_rate_2534 measures model ages 26/30/34, old_age_own_rate measures 66/70/74; whether that drops 65 and adds 75-77 depends on the bin-vs-point age convention of the data moments — these carry weights 80 and 160); fert_by_age/mean_age_first_birth are computed on POST-fertility-split childless mass (biased diagnostic, not in the active target set); the non-markov KFE defines housing_increment_0to1_eventstudy_t3 differently (post-minus-pre, no control cohort) from the active markov KFE (DiD vs childless control) under the same moment name; and replaying pre-repair records with stored switches reproduces old timing by design. The repair test file only proves helper-level unit behavior on synthetic fixtures — it never tests the end-to-end pipeline — but my identity test now closes that gap. The local tmp snapshot copy of the solver's markov KFE section and both flag files is byte-identical to the audited tree; cluster-side confirmation remains blocked (SSH down).
summaries.v2:e7c90d5c435034b5221c531d4887a23e7bae9: Finance/supply audit complete; the implemented finance block is internally consistent and the combined-spec overrides all land correctly, but there are two MAJOR issues. (1) The paper and code disagree on the owner service flow: the code implements chi*(H - hbar) (kernels.py:915-918), while the live full draft (tex:509+521) specifies services chi_O*H_k entering (h - hbar), i.e. chi*H - hbar; at the candidate theta this changes the owner housing surplus by up to ~2x on small rungs and changes rung feasibility. (2) In current_bound_best the searched supply scale H0=9.999672 sits AT its upper bound 10; at the equilibrium price the supply factor (uc/r_bar)^1.75=0.555 means the rooms target 5.780 needs H0=10.41, so the rooms moment (model 5.551, gap -0.229, loss contribution 0.314) cannot discipline H0 and the calibration is a bound-constrained optimum, traceable to r_bar=0.16 being a pivot inherited from the old finance regime. Defaults in parameters.py remain the OLD finance parameters (annual 4%/2%: q=0.169859, delta=0.077632, user-cost 0.28749 vs combined 0.16571 — 1.73x), and production_profile_overrides() does NOT carry q/delta/eta_supply/H0, so any tool run without the combined fixed spec silently evaluates the old regime. Everything else checks out numerically at Nb=32 with the exact overnight override chain: (1+q) applies symmetrically to assets and debt (mortgage rate = deposit rate); down payment is (1-phi)pH with borrowing limit -phi pH and no code path uses phi as the DP share; the uniform owner debt floor binds exactly at -phi*p*H for every rung including stayers; renter b'>=0 and the 6-room renter cap and [2,4,6,8,10] owner menu are in force (max renter policy exactly 6.0); rent=(q+delta+tau_H)p, owner flow cost (delta+tau_H)pH, supply H0*(uc/0.16)^1.75 all match the paper's user-cost/supply equations, and the supply identity holds on all four saved repro records to within the reported residual (~3e-5). The rooms moment is post-decision occupied rooms per household over ages 18-85 and equals aggregate demand/total mass exactly, consistent with the July 9 timing repair. Minor items: the paper's incumbent no-delever LTV carve-out (b' >= min{b,-phi P H}) is not implemented (vacuous at stationary prices, not in price-raising counterfactuals); bequest base is gross b+pH vs the paper's "after liquidation costs" (cross-flag to audit B); tau_H is linearly aggregated while q/delta compound; and the lead's spec sheet constant delta4=0.04328066 is slightly wrong (exact 0.043279309359). The dirty-tree one-liner adding .reshape(-1) to the H0 override (parameters.py:299) is load-bearing for the scalar searched H0 — the overnight run required the dirty tree.
summaries.v2:7e45fe77eb941e328f72e3b6bde9d27870cb6: Data-side target audit of all 15 moments is complete; worksheet at output/model/full_audit_20260711/target_audit/TARGET_DATA_AUDIT.md. Bottom line: the target system is in much better provenance shape than the July 2 audit suggested — 13 of 15 point values are byte-exact reproducible from in-repo builders plus microdata, largely because an untracked bootstrap-SE harness (code/data/moment_standard_errors/, built July 5-6) already reran every builder filter and reproduced 12/14 targets to <5e-7 with a hard gate. The two fertility targets (tfr=1.918, childless_rate=0.188) have no in-repo builder; they were pinned July 8 to a single published CPS June 2024 Table H2 cell (women 40-44 CEB=1,918/1,000; 18.8% childless) — exact and wave-consistent, but citation-level only (no raw artifact checked in). The NEW 15th target, aggregate_mean_occupied_rooms_18_85=5.779970481941968, is generated by an UNCOMMITTED working-tree edit to the room-target builder, regenerated ~3h before the overnight, matches the overnight driver constant byte-exactly, and is NOT national ACS — it is the same 42-metro MMS matched-sample of household heads (18-85, literal ROOMS, HHWT) as the other room targets, and its own CSV status says "candidate; re-audit before SMM use". Cross-target consistency is good within blocks but the room block (heads=PERNUM==1, all structures) and the ownership block (heads=PERNUM==1 & RELATE==1, UNITSSTR 3:10) use different head and structure universes; PSID wealth targets are national all-individual samples (couples double-counted) with annual INCFAMR denominators and untrimmed negative wealth. The previously flagged period-vs-annual and ratio-of-sums model-data mismatches are FIXED in the code the overnight ran (annual_gross_income_at_state; mean-of-cell-ratios), and the target-object audit doc is stale on this point. The most economically important finding: the bootstrap SE file implies inverse-variance weights that differ from the overnight's ad hoc weights by up to ~500x in relative terms (the noisiest moment, the old-age parent-childless wealth gap with SE=68% of its target, is nearly optimally weighted while own_family_gap is relatively downweighted ~500x), so the estimated point is efficiency-arbitrary and no inference can be hung on the loss; the SE file existed four days before the overnight and was not used. old_age_own_rate is ACS/MMS DUE-housing heads (PSID cross-check 0.8637 does not match), answering the lead's open question.
summaries.v2:5f4ed30c06ad2694b39e6de7c8cb5a6c52c87: The five-state Rouwenhorst income process in the July-10 combined spec is implemented correctly: the recursion, grid spacing (psi = sigma_log*sqrt(N-1)), binomial stationary weights, exact 4-year aggregation (rho_period = rho_annual^4 = 0.85 exactly; sigma_eps4^2 = sigma_eps1^2 * sum rho^{2k}), and level normalization E[z]=1 under the invariant weights all check out numerically, and the constructed objects' fingerprint (5b6875a2...) matches the run snapshot bit-for-bit. The headline substantive finding is that the "matched annual AR(1)" is a pure re-parameterization of the old ad hoc grid: rho_annual equals 0.85^(1/4) to machine zero and the stationary log variance equals the old grid's weighted log variance (0.0533671307381197) to 3e-17. The paper discloses the matching, but the resulting persistent-income risk (stationary log sd 0.231) is roughly half the repo's own Sommer-Sullivan reference (0.459) and 2-3.5x below standard literature calibrations (Floden-Linde, STY, KMP stationary log sd ~0.46-0.80) — the dispersion is inherited from a grid that never had an empirical source, which matters for the five wealth-to-income and income-gradient targets and for policy counterfactuals. Ages 18-21 correctly receive the 0.650 first-break value (step profile, normalized to 0.7206 of the working mean), not the legacy peak; a single P.income array feeds the Bellman, KFE, moment denominators, entry-wealth scaling, and the pension. Retirement starts at j=12 (age 66); pension is z-independent and identical in Bellman and KFE; the 65-75 target block covers only retired ages so its z=1.0 shortcut is innocuous. Entry z is drawn from the stationary binomial weights and the same Pi_z drives the Bellman expectation and the forward distribution. Leak surfaces: the production profile still ships the OLD grid and the profile validator does not check the income process (the combined spec survives only via merge order, verified correct for the overnight); CLI defaults are "current" (or SS 0.90/0.20 if rouwenhorst is chosen bare); and eight checked-in diagnostic/policy tools (including run_intergen_policy_poc.py and the sensitivity Jacobian) hard-default to the old process, so post-overnight policy or sensitivity numbers computed with them would silently not match the estimated model. A legacy mean-income denominator bug (entry-age income used as worker mean) exists but is overwritten in the markov path used by production. Cluster-side metadata checks were blocked (SSH down) but are mitigated by the exact fingerprint and the lead's exact loss reproduction.
summaries.v2:0adf1baa64c90e59dca2d0cf24b5b493adbc6: Diagnostics packet built and inspected for the current-bound candidate (GE re-solve at Nb=120 reproduced the loss exactly: 14.780020699972585, residual 2.906e-05, p_eq 0.6897624769313723) plus a light PE pass on the relaxed candidate; standard package plot set written via diagnostics.write_diagnostics, no new graphs. Mechanics are largely clean: mass accounting is exact (total 1.0, per-age mass uniform at 1/17 to 5e-16), no bunching at the wealth-grid edges, no negative consumption, market clears strictly. But two linked pathologies contaminate the fertility block. First, the PSID entry-wealth distribution injects entrants over 27 wealth nodes in [-2.91, +3.51], and at the fixed floor c_bar_0=1.28 about 20.4% of each entering cohort lands on nodes the Bellman marks infeasible (V=-1e10); 1.72% of total population mass lives on such nodes and transits through the KFE on default policies. Second, at fully infeasible childless nodes the fertility logit degenerates to exactly (1/3,1/3,1/3) (logsumexp over three -1e10 values), so roughly 0.012 births per cohort — about 21% of ALL model births — are mechanical artifacts at age 18-22; TFR net of the j=0 artifact is roughly 1.57 versus the reported 1.983 (target 1.918), so the near-perfect TFR/childlessness fit is partly an artifact composition. Separately, 40% of childless fertile-window mass (all on feasible nodes, mean wealth ~0) has birth probability exactly zero because the birth option is infeasible — the extensive fertility margin is feasibility-driven, not smoothed choice. Value-function "monotonicity violations" (168 pairwise drops) are sentinel contamination: -1e10 propagates through expectations, leaving 0.57% of mass on nodes with V < -1e6. The relaxed candidate shows the same pathologies at similar magnitude (invalid mass 1.74%, p_birth=0 share 43.2%). Cluster-side checks remain blocked (SSH unavailable). Full report: output/model/full_audit_20260711/diagnostics/DIAGNOSTICS_REPORT.md.

v2:d6b8d721ec5a382f7b2bc4a79357c777b816f.item: Strict gating: no non-strict record can be selected in-run, by select_seed, or by the reducer
v2:d6b8d721ec5a382f7b2bc4a79357c777b816f.note: record_selection_loss returns math.inf unless strict_converged (1151-1155); is_better_record requires finite loss (1132-1138); strict_converged in run_local_panel_case (863-867) embeds residual<=tol_eq; select_seed filters strict_record (run_overnight_combined.py:53); reducer eligible = strict rows only (reduce_results.py:45-53). A lower-loss non-strict record is IGNORED (not a crash); RuntimeError fires only when zero strict records exist (run_wave2_lateral.py:116-117,155-156; run_overnight_combined.py:168-169) — corrects the lead's framing slightly.

v2:d6b8d721ec5a382f7b2bc4a79357c777b816f.item: Override merge order: income wins over the profile's stale z-grid in BOTH wave-2 code paths (global stage and polish)
v2:d6b8d721ec5a382f7b2bc4a79357c777b816f.note: overrides = {**base_overrides, **(extra_overrides or {}), **income, **theta}. Wave-2 global stage builds extra = production_profile_overrides() then .update(q/delta/eta_supply/normalize_bequest) (run_wave2_lateral.py:68-76) and passes income separately (line 99 of the call); polish path identical via profile_extra_overrides (local_panel.py:565-579, 670-681). theta (incl. searched H0) merges last; no key collisions between fixed spec and income.

v2:d6b8d721ec5a382f7b2bc4a79357c777b816f.item: Report parameter tables list the ACTUAL per-arm search bounds (patched for the relaxed arm), and the beta row is the annual/period transform, not an out-of-bounds estimate
v2:d6b8d721ec5a382f7b2bc4a79357c777b816f.note: housing CSV shows chi [0.4,1.6], h_bar_0 [0.25,6], h_bar_jump [0.05,3.5], h_bar_n [0.02,3.0]; current CSV shows production bounds + H0 [1,10]. beta estimate 0.8411423842686492 = 0.9576732996982973^4 exactly (verified; theta_from_global_unit local_panel.py:939-940 'theta["beta"] = value**PERIOD_YEARS'); bound_scale_estimate is annual and interior to [0.94,0.995].

v2:d6b8d721ec5a382f7b2bc4a79357c777b816f.item: Wave-1 seeding design: distinct deterministic jittered starts per task; jitter=0 reproduces the seed exactly
v2:d6b8d721ec5a382f7b2bc4a79357c777b816f.note: jitter_theta uses np.random.default_rng(task_seed) with task seeds 2026071100+TASK_ID (run_array.sh:41); verified deterministic, distinct across seeds, identity at jitter=0. Consequence: 4 jitter=0 tasks (one per arm) share one start; 21 distinct wave-1 starts total, all within jitter<=0.15 of one seed. Jitter ladder (0,0.01,0.025,0.05,0.09,0.15) at run_array.sh:31; arms x slots = 24 tasks (array=1-24 supplied at submit time; no #SBATCH --array in the script).

v2:d6b8d721ec5a382f7b2bc4a79357c777b816f.item: Wave-2 budget arithmetic: 92.4-min global deadline comfortably covers 60 draws at 37-74 s/solve
v2:d6b8d721ec5a382f7b2bc4a79357c777b816f.note: min(100, 330*0.28)=92.4 min vs ~37 min (37 s/solve) or ~74 min (74 s/solve) for 60 draws; the seed (idx 0) is always evaluated. Truncation only if average case >~92 s (see finding S5 for the residual uncertainty).

v2:d6b8d721ec5a382f7b2bc4a79357c777b816f.item: Reducer aggregation shape matches wave output layout
v2:d6b8d721ec5a382f7b2bc4a79357c777b816f.note: Single-level glob '*/task_*/best.json' matches task-level best.json written by run_local_polish (wave 1) and by run_wave2_lateral.py:157 (wave 2 final best); cross_arm_best and arm_best computed over strict rows only; non-strict rows retained but ineligible.

v2:95d5aa7e45e92337acd5b7b77e264a1d6e5b5.item: Objective form and exact loss reproduction for both candidates
v2:95d5aa7e45e92337acd5b7b77e264a1d6e5b5.note: diagnostic_loss = sum_i weights[name]*(value-target)^2 in raw units, no normalization; +100 penalty only if market_residual non-finite or > 5e-3 (inactive at residuals 2.9e-05/3.1e-05); recompute_loss.py reproduces 14.780020699972585 and 13.90346531862862 with diff 0.0 from stored fit arrays AND from calibration.py constants; every stored gap = model-target and contribution = w*gap^2 exactly. housing_relaxed stored contribution_sum differs from stored loss by 1 ulp (summation order) only.

v2:95d5aa7e45e92337acd5b7b77e264a1d6e5b5.item: Active target/weight vector matches the code-side target set plus the overnight extra target
v2:95d5aa7e45e92337acd5b7b77e264a1d6e5b5.note: candidate_replacement_post_audit_v1 resolves to exactly the 14 stored moments/targets/weights (tfr 1.918@20, childless 0.188@20, own_rate 0.57547241@100, own_family_gap 0.16766167@45, hi0to1 0.66443467@14, old NH median 2.23046078@0.8, old NH gap 1.00744952@2, renter rooms 3.80528810@6, owner ge6 0.59613112@25, young wealth 0.17922556@12, roomgap 2.41876173@12, old own 0.76426097@160, own2534 0.34116609@80, 3plus rooms 0.36769955881@8) plus aggregate_mean_occupied_rooms_18_85 5.779970481941968@6 injected via additional_targets/additional_weights (local_panel.py:558-561 enforces key equality).

v2:95d5aa7e45e92337acd5b7b77e264a1d6e5b5.item: rank_loss recorded by the evaluation path equals diagnostic_loss and selection is strict-gated
v2:95d5aa7e45e92337acd5b7b77e264a1d6e5b5.note: run_local_panel_case: 'rank_loss = diagnostic_loss(moments, targets=rank_targets, weights=rank_weights)'; record_selection_loss returns inf unless strict_converged (solver strict flag AND residual <= tol_eq=1e-4), so stored best records carry penalty-free rank_loss.

v2:95d5aa7e45e92337acd5b7b77e264a1d6e5b5.item: Fertility/completed-fertility windows have no overlap and match the stated lifecycle
v2:95d5aa7e45e92337acd5b7b77e264a1d6e5b5.note: Births decided for j+1 in [A_f_start,A_f_end]=[1,7] -> ages 18-42; parity_dist/mean_parity/childless_rate measured on g[..., A_f_end:, ...] = ages 46-82 (uniform age mass, N_target=1, no mortality). tfr = 2*mean_parity is the documented completed-fertility-equivalent convention (calibration.py:207-212), coherent with the M14 parity-bin mapping (nn=1 -> data 1-2 kids, nn=2 -> data 3+).

v2:95d5aa7e45e92337acd5b7b77e264a1d6e5b5.item: Age-window arithmetic on the 18+4j grid, including retirement alignment
v2:95d5aa7e45e92337acd5b7b77e264a1d6e5b5.note: age_to_index rounds with no .5 ties hit: 25->2, 30->3, 34->4, 35->4, 45->7, 55->9, 65->12, 75->14; J_R=12 (age 66) so the 65-75 wealth/ownership window is entirely retired and the hard-coded z=1.0 at solver.py:4896 is innocuous (income_at_state ignores z for j>=J_R, solver.py:127-131).

v2:95d5aa7e45e92337acd5b7b77e264a1d6e5b5.item: Income denominators for the wealth moments
v2:95d5aa7e45e92337acd5b7b77e264a1d6e5b5.note: annual_gross_income_at_state = period income/period_years, grossed up by 1/(1-tau_pay) for workers only; M10 uses z-resolved annual gross labor income on the income-resolved asset distribution (asset_current = beginning-of-period b conditioned on current tenure, solver.py:4030-4041,4094); M6/M7 use annual pension. Wealth is liquid b only (negatives included); home equity (1-psi)*p*H_own enters only 'total' variants.

v2:95d5aa7e45e92337acd5b7b77e264a1d6e5b5.item: Renter room threshold moments computed pre-collapse (Jensen fix in place)
v2:95d5aa7e45e92337acd5b7b77e264a1d6e5b5.note: markov_renter_room_moments recomputes prime30_55_childless_renter_share_rooms_ge6 and the renter median on the full income-resolved state and overwrites the collapsed versions; mean-rooms moments are exact under the mass-weighted policy collapse (collapse_markov_policy num/den construction, solver.py:4098-4105), so M8/M11 are unaffected by the collapse.

aggregate_housing_demand = sum(g_current * hR) for renters + sum(g_current)*H_own per rung, normalizer N_target=1; extract_moments divides by total_mass = sum(g_current) (calibration.py:1090-1092, added in the dirty tree), giving mean occupied rooms per household over all 17 periods (ages 18-86 vs the 18-85 data label); same demand object used for market clearing, so the moment is measured on the market-clearing distribution.

v2:95d5aa7e45e92337acd5b7b77e264a1d6e5b5.item: housing_increment_0to1 event-study (markov path) uses birth-minus-control at horizon 0
v2:95d5aa7e45e92337acd5b7b77e264a1d6e5b5.note: (birth_es3_post_sum - birth_es3_control_post_sum)/birth_es3_mass: birth cohort and a same-mass childless control advanced housing_event_horizon=0 periods, realized post-decision; matches the June 25 fixed definition in the ledger (calibration.py:231-236). Note the non-markov path (solver.py:3659-3661) still uses post-minus-pre, but the overnight ran the markov path.

v2:95d5aa7e45e92337acd5b7b77e264a1d6e5b5.item: Dirty-tree changes touching this audit's scope are limited and well-formed
v2:95d5aa7e45e92337acd5b7b77e264a1d6e5b5.note: git diff HEAD shows only: extract_moments adds aggregate_mean_occupied_rooms_18_85; parameters.py reshapes H0 override to 1-D and adds normalize_bequest_utility default False; solver.py adds the bequest normalization branch. No dirty-tree change alters any target value, weight, window, or the loss function.

v2:107ca8423aa3f26fb62504882b6a3418ef938.item: Rouwenhorst income process: 5 states, annual rho 0.960/sd 0.0645, rho_4y=0.8500; paper grid {0.613,0.773,0.974,1.227,1.546} and weights (0.0625,0.25,0.375,0.25,0.0625) reproduce the code construction exactly; mean-one grid normalization
v2:107ca8423aa3f26fb62504882b6a3418ef938.note: Recomputed grid from the driver constants (MATCHED_ANNUAL_RHO/SD in run_overnight_combined.py:25-26) matches paper lines 444-449 and 907-910 to displayed precision

v2:107ca8423aa3f26fb62504882b6a3418ef938.item: Bequest function: normalized form theta0(1+theta_n n)[((theta1+max{W,0})^{1-sigma}-theta1^{1-sigma})/(1-sigma)] on gross estate b+Ph, no transaction cost or tax at death; estate_tax_rate=0 not overridden
v2:107ca8423aa3f26fb62504882b6a3418ef938.note: 'utility = utility - P.theta1 ** (1 - P.sigma) / (1 - P.sigma)' under normalize flag; 'Vbq[...] = bequest_utility_vec(b_grid + hv, nk, P)' with hv = p_hat*H_own; matches paper eq. (quant_bequest) lines 503-513

v2:107ca8423aa3f26fb62504882b6a3418ef938.item: Finance block: q override (1.02)^4-1, delta=1-(1-0.011)^4, tau_H=0.04, phi=0.80 financed share, down payment (1-phi)PH, debt floor -phi PH, sale proceeds (1-psi)Ph with psi=0.06
v2:107ca8423aa3f26fb62504882b6a3418ef938.note: 'dp_arr[...] = (1 - phi_ncs) * hcost' and 'bmo[...] = -phi_ncs * hcost'; paper eqs (quant_dp)/(quant_ltv)/(quant_sale_proceeds) lines 555-577 match; annual conversions at paper lines 893-900 match code

v2:107ca8423aa3f26fb62504882b6a3418ef938.item: Owner housing services use the residual form chi*(H - hbar(n,s)), as displayed in paper eq. (quant_housing_services)
v2:107ca8423aa3f26fb62504882b6a3418ef938.note: 'ht_c = hsv - owner_h_bar_scale * hbc ... ht_c = owner_service_premium * ht_c' with owner_service_premium=P.chi (solver.py:2012) and owner_h_bar_scale=1.0 default; July 9 paper decision implemented

v2:107ca8423aa3f26fb62504882b6a3418ef938.item: Supply closure: H^S = H0*(r/r_bar)^eta with eta=1.75 and H0 searched in [1,10] against ACS target 5.779970481941968 (weight 6)
v2:107ca8423aa3f26fb62504882b6a3418ef938.note: 'Hs[i] = P.H0[i] * (r[i] / P.r_bar[i]) ** P.xi_supply[i]'; spec override eta_supply=[1.75] and additional target in run_overnight_combined.py:156-162; paper lines 527-533, 961, 975-977 consistent

v2:107ca8423aa3f26fb62504882b6a3418ef938.item: Menu and lifecycle configuration: H_own {2,4,6,8,10}, renter cap 6, J=17 four-year periods from age 18 (last 82-85), retirement 66, fertile window 18-42, child maturity prob 2/9, E_0=1/J, balanced pension, wealth grid [-12,30] with core [-5,7]
v2:107ca8423aa3f26fb62504882b6a3418ef938.note: Also calibration.py:17-21 (age constants) and parameters.py:424-431 (pension); paper computation section lines 886-917 and outside-estimation table all match

v2:107ca8423aa3f26fb62504882b6a3418ef938.item: Units crosswalk disclosed: family-size index n in {0,1,2}, n=1 is 1-2 children, n=2 is 3+, two children per index unit, CPS completed fertility reported as 2E[n]; code tfr = 2.0*mean_completed_fertility

15-moment/14-parameter system: paper fit table lists exactly the candidate_replacement_post_audit_v1 targets plus aggregate_mean_occupied_rooms_18_85 with correct target values; parameter table has all 14 searched parameters (13 production bounds + H0)
v2:107ca8423aa3f26fb62504882b6a3418ef938.note: All 15 target values match the code target set; all 15 model moments, both parameter tables, residual 2.39e-5, mechanism numbers (0.178/0.130/0.307/2.37x), family-stock shares, and mean age at first birth 30.9065 match the frozen circulation candidate packet exactly

v2:107ca8423aa3f26fb62504882b6a3418ef938.item: Entrant wealth distribution from PSID (childless renters 25-35, quintile nodes of NETWORTH2R/INCFAMR) active in the evaluation path via base_overrides
v2:107ca8423aa3f26fb62504882b6a3418ef938.note: 'entry_wealth_mode": "income_ratio_distribution"' with 5 PSID nodes; paper outside-estimation table row (lines 963-964) is accurate

v2:107ca8423aa3f26fb62504882b6a3418ef938.item: Paper figures are byte-identical to the frozen candidate packet copies
v2:107ca8423aa3f26fb62504882b6a3418ef938.note: SHA1 98a5a117... and d66fae23... (decision rules) match output/model/intergen_circulation_candidate_20260711 copies; produced under repaired post-decision timing (use_postdecision_current_distribution=True)

v2:fd84ea524e7efcb52f1df5ed3601c1ad80472.item: Bellman flow utility at age j comes from the newly chosen tenure/housing and the same-period child state; nesting order matches the KFE
v2:fd84ea524e7efcb52f1df5ed3601c1ad80472.note: Renter branch chooses hR at rent ri; owner branch consumes H_own[tn-1] this period ('ht_o = owner_service_premium * np.maximum(hsv - owner_h_bar_scale * SD.hb_flat, 1e-10)'); tenure choice evaluates Vd at transaction wealth with (1-phi) down-payment check at 2717-2731; fertility chooses (nn, cs=1) contemporaneously at 2784.

v2:fd84ea524e7efcb52f1df5ed3601c1ad80472.item: Post-decision distribution g_current is the single object used for moments AND market clearing in every equilibrium branch (damped loop, bracket, Brent refine, final full solve)
v2:fd84ea524e7efcb52f1df5ed3601c1ad80472.note: Decisive test T5: demand recomputed from the returned distribution equals sol.housing_demand to 1.8e-15; the new aggregate_mean_occupied_rooms_18_85 target is the same object divided by total_mass = sum(g_current).

v2:fd84ea524e7efcb52f1df5ed3601c1ad80472.item: Mass conservation of realize_current_cross_section age-by-age and by (age,n,cs)
v2:fd84ea524e7efcb52f1df5ed3601c1ad80472.note: Max gap 8.7e-17 on a solved tiny model (J=17, Nb=28, 2 z-states); the in-solver assert (solver.py:4042-4045) is aggregate-only atol=1e-10, so this closes a real gap.

v2:fd84ea524e7efcb52f1df5ed3601c1ad80472.item: Contemporaneous-tenure identity: reported own-by-age equals manual application of the age-j tenure policy to age-j beginning mass; legacy arm is exactly the one-period lag
v2:fd84ea524e7efcb52f1df5ed3601c1ad80472.note: Max deviation 1.5e-15 at every age; max_j |own_pre - own_post| = 0.152 confirms the test has power and characterizes the pre-repair defect exactly as the July 9 report described.

v2:fd84ea524e7efcb52f1df5ed3601c1ad80472.item: Birth timing: n, child costs, and housing floor bind at j; birth-triggered same-period purchases counted in the horizon-0 event-study DiD and stationary moments
v2:fd84ea524e7efcb52f1df5ed3601c1ad80472.note: advance_cohort_horizon with horizon 0 is the identity; decisive T4 shows newborn-state owner share 0.0059 post vs 0.0002 inherited at the birth age in the toy model.

v2:fd84ea524e7efcb52f1df5ed3601c1ad80472.item: Forward transaction map mirrors Bellman accounting including grant/waiver precedence, borrowing floor, and sale nonnegativity
v2:fd84ea524e7efcb52f1df5ed3601c1ad80472.note: 'branch_wealth = np.clip(np.maximum(bg - purchase_cost + grant, -financed_share * purchase_cost), bmin, bmax)'; unit tests 349-426 of test_code_repair_20260709.py cover the map math; feasibility flooring never binds for argmax-feasible mass since b >= (1-phi)hc implies b - hc >= -phi*hc.

v2:fd84ea524e7efcb52f1df5ed3601c1ad80472.item: Flag defaults True; overnight drivers inherited the repaired switches; local snapshot copy byte-identical
v2:fd84ea524e7efcb52f1df5ed3601c1ad80472.note: production_profile_overrides pins the switches (production_profile.py:159-166); run_local_polish merge order local_panel.py:565-579, 848-853 cannot be overridden by income/theta keys; diff of solver.py lines 3683-4096 vs tmp/intergen_combined_specification_20260710 copy is empty.

v2:fd84ea524e7efcb52f1df5ed3601c1ad80472.item: Wealth moments' declared beginning-of-period-b-conditional-on-current-choice convention implemented as stated
v2:fd84ea524e7efcb52f1df5ed3601c1ad80472.note: assign_current_choices_to_beginning_assets evaluates the tenure decision at transaction wealth but records mass at the original b coordinate; used for all liquid/old-age wealth ratio moments including the targeted young_childless_renter_liquid_wealth_to_annual_gross_income_2535.

v2:fd84ea524e7efcb52f1df5ed3601c1ad80472.item: Renter demand integrand coordinate consistency: just-sold renters land at max(b+sale,0), exactly where the Bellman evaluated the renter policy
v2:fd84ea524e7efcb52f1df5ed3601c1ad80472.note: So g_current[:,0]*hR_pol[:,0] measures the housing actually chosen up to linear interpolation.

Proves helper-level map math, same-period birth+purchase visibility on a 1-point synthetic cohort, terminal-age realization, beginning-asset conditioning, one-period advance semantics, and profile pinning. Does NOT prove anything end-to-end (moments==clearing object, age-by-age conservation, DiD construction, window indices, Bellman flow timing); the audit identity test closes those gaps.

v2:e7c90d5c435034b5221c531d4887a23e7bae9.item: Combined finance overrides land and propagate: q=0.08243216, delta=0.04327930936, user_cost_rate and R_gross recomputed after every override merge
v2:e7c90d5c435034b5221c531d4887a23e7bae9.note: Verified in tiny Nb=32 solve through the exact overnight merge order {base_overrides, **profile+fixed, **income, **theta}: P.q/P.delta/P.eta_supply/P.xi_supply/P.H0 all exact.

v2:e7c90d5c435034b5221c531d4887a23e7bae9.item: (1+q) applies symmetrically to liquid assets and mortgage debt; mortgage rate = deposit rate; q used nowhere else active (PTI path off)
v2:e7c90d5c435034b5221c531d4887a23e7bae9.note: b grid spans negative (mortgage) positions; single gross return Rg on both signs.

v2:e7c90d5c435034b5221c531d4887a23e7bae9.item: Down-payment threshold (1-phi)*pH and borrowing limit -phi*pH; owner-to-owner uses dp-(1-psi)pH_old; no code path uses phi as the DP share
v2:e7c90d5c435034b5221c531d4887a23e7bae9.note: Buyer feasibility 'if bg_b < dpn or bab < bmn: v_tn = NEG_INF' is the correct equivalent pair; phi=0.80 confirmed on P in the combined chain.

v2:e7c90d5c435034b5221c531d4887a23e7bae9.item: Uniform owner debt floor -phi*p*H binds for all owners including stayers; renter b'>=0
v2:e7c90d5c435034b5221c531d4887a23e7bae9.note: Numerically: min bp_pol per rung equals -phi*p*H to 1e-15 at Nb=32; states never begin below the floor at stationary p.

v2:e7c90d5c435034b5221c531d4887a23e7bae9.item: Rental rate r=(q+delta+tau_H)p; owner flow cost (delta+tau_H)pH; matches paper eq quant_user_cost and the owner-budget text
v2:e7c90d5c435034b5221c531d4887a23e7bae9.lines: 1252 ('r = P.user_cost_rate * p'), 2056/2421 ('ocst[i, ten] = (P.delta + P.tau_H) * p_hat[i] * hs + extra_size_cost')
v2:e7c90d5c435034b5221c531d4887a23e7bae9.note: Interest component of owner user cost flows through R_gross on b; no stock depreciation state (rungs fixed, maintenance restores).

v2:e7c90d5c435034b5221c531d4887a23e7bae9.item: Supply H_s = H0*((q+delta+tau_H)p/r_bar)^eta, one pooled renter+owner market per location, residual metric |excess|/supply with tol 1e-4
v2:e7c90d5c435034b5221c531d4887a23e7bae9.lines: 5484 ('supply = P.H0 * (user_cost / P.r_bar) ** P.xi_supply'), 1025 (supply-inverse price target), 1100-1104 (refine metric), 4566-4575 (demand aggregation)
v2:e7c90d5c435034b5221c531d4887a23e7bae9.note: Supply identity verified on all four saved repro records to 2.7e-5..6.6e-5 (= reported residuals); eta_supply array indexing is a benign I=1 location remnant; units are mean rooms per household (N_target=1 normalization).

v2:e7c90d5c435034b5221c531d4887a23e7bae9.item: Owner menu [2,4,6,8,10] and renter cap 6.0 in force in the overnight evaluation chain and the reported candidates
v2:e7c90d5c435034b5221c531d4887a23e7bae9.note: Tiny solve: P.H_own=[2,4,6,8,10], P.hR_max=6.0, max renter rooms policy exactly 6.0 (cap binds), owner slots of hR_pol all zero.

v2:e7c90d5c435034b5221c531d4887a23e7bae9.item: aggregate_mean_occupied_rooms_18_85 = post-decision occupied rooms per household over all 17 periods (ages 18-85): (renter hR + owner rung H, weighted by g_current) / total_mass
v2:e7c90d5c435034b5221c531d4887a23e7bae9.note: g_current = realize_current_cross_section (use_postdecision_current_distribution=True in the production profile), timing stamped 'post_housing_choice' — consistent with the July 9 repair; identity rooms == demand/mass verified exactly in the tiny solve.

v2:e7c90d5c435034b5221c531d4887a23e7bae9.item: Dirty-tree H0 override reshape is load-bearing: 'P.H0 = np.asarray(P.H0, dtype=float).reshape(-1)'

v2:5f4ed30c06ad2694b39e6de7c8cb5a6c52c87.item: Rouwenhorst recursion, symmetric p=(1+rho)/2, binomial stationary weights, row sums; stationary eigenvector equals comb(4,k)/16 to 2e-16; first-order log autocorrelation exactly 0.85

4-year aggregation math: rho_period = rho_annual^4 = 0.8500000000000001; sigma_eps_period = sd_annual*sqrt(sum_{k=0..3} rho^{2k}) = 0.121694; sigma_log = 0.231013; grid endpoints +/- sigma_log*sqrt(N-1); discrete stationary log var equals sigma_log^2 to 2e-17
v2:5f4ed30c06ad2694b39e6de7c8cb5a6c52c87.note: Exact time-aggregation of an annual AR(1) sampled every 4 years; correctly implemented

v2:5f4ed30c06ad2694b39e6de7c8cb5a6c52c87.item: Level normalization: z_grid divided by (z_weights @ z_grid) once at construction; stationary level mean exactly 1; log mean is -0.02662 (not zero) by design; nothing re-normalizes downstream
v2:5f4ed30c06ad2694b39e6de7c8cb5a6c52c87.note: parameters.py:300-320 renormalizes weights and Pi rows only, never the grid level

v2:5f4ed30c06ad2694b39e6de7c8cb5a6c52c87.item: Override merge order gives the Rouwenhorst objects precedence over the production profile's stale z-grid; P.z_grid after apply_overrides equals the Rouwenhorst grid (checked end-to-end through apply_overrides)
